chunks.title: CONTROL Tier — Detailed Definition
chunks.content:
CONTROL — Full Platform, Hard + Soft, Complete Visibility

Includes everything in ELEVATE, PLUS:
- Full Hard Pull access (bundled, not transactional)
- Complete credit visibility across all bureaus
- Credit Guardrails for BOTH hard pulls AND soft pulls

Job To Be Done: Cover all bases, close deals faster with certainty, and put control back into the hands of dealership leadership.

Best Fit (ICP for Control):
- Risk-averse dealerships that desire efficiency AND control AND require traditional credit access
- Multi-rooftop groups and high-volume dealers requiring full-platform control across all locations
- Dealers who run heavy hard-pull volumes (where transactional pricing in Elevate would exceed bundled Control pricing)
- Dealers who want the most defensible, fully-instrumented operation

Value Message:
"Complete visibility. Complete control. Fraud stopped. Deals funded. Compliance locked."

Persona Hooks:
- CEO/Dealer Principal: Enterprise-level visibility and accountability across all rooftops
- CFO: Full cost predictability + fraud loss prevention = margin protection
- GSM: Complete showroom accountability + conversion rate lift
- F&I Director: Full deal funding optimization — STIPs, soft pull, payment desking unified
- Compliance Officer: Board-ready compliance reporting + complete audit trail across rooftops

Elevate vs Control decision rule: If the dealer runs >5 hard pulls per delivered car (rule of thumb), Control's bundled pricing is more economical. If they run <3, Elevate's transactional pricing wins. 3-5 is the gray zone where you model both.

chunks.title: ELEVATE Tier — Detailed Definition
chunks.content:
ELEVATE — Control Credit Spend, Reduce Compliance Burden, Add Soft Pulls

Includes everything in ENRICH (which includes Protect + SmartPencil), PLUS:
- Soft-Pull-powered workflows (PRIORITIZED — this is the soft-pull tier)
- Credit Spend Controls: platform optimizes credit spend by controlling the showroom-floor process
- RBPN, OFAC, AA letters, and all credit-required compliance (same as Protect)
- Transactional hard pulls (pay-per-use, not bundled flat-rate)
- Customer Insights dashboard
- Liens & Judgements Summary
- Income & Employment Verification

Job To Be Done:
- Control credit spend (especially for dealers priced out of the market by soft pull cost increases)
- Reduce compliance burden via soft-pull-first workflows
- Minimize sales friction and improve velocity
- Reduce overall credit spend by replacing bundled hard pulls with transactional hard pulls

Best Fit (ICP for Elevate):
- COST-CONSCIOUS multi-rooftop dealer groups looking to reduce credit spend
- Dealers who can no longer afford to do hard pulls and soft pulls together at full bundled rate
- Dealers who have been priced out by soft pull price increases at competitors
- Dealers wanting to move credit decisions earlier in the funnel (pre-desk)

Value Message:
"Sell smarter. Know your buyer before the desk. Control your costs while you do it."

Persona Hooks:
- CEO/Dealer Principal: Cost control + revenue quality improvement
- CFO: Hard pull reduction via soft pull strategy; Guardrails limit overspend
- GSM: Pre-qualified buyers entering the desk; fewer wasted deals
- F&I Director: Fewer recontracts, faster funding, soft-pull pre-qualification
- Compliance Officer: Soft pull reduces compliance burden on high-volume credit activity

Critical distinction from Control: Elevate uses TRANSACTIONAL hard pulls (you pay per hard pull). Control includes FULL hard-pull access bundled.

chunks.title: ENRICH Tier — Detailed Definition
chunks.content:
ENRICH — Structure Better Deals and Protect Profit

Includes everything in PROTECT, PLUS:
- SmartPencil deal structuring engine
- Automated profit optimization across hundreds of lending permutations

What SmartPencil Does (inside Enrich):
- Calculates all lending scenarios instantly
- Auto-selects best payment with highest profit
- Considers residuals, incentives, lender rules, buy rate, markup limits, splits, fees, TTL
- Dynamic adjustments: change any input, payments update instantly
- Embedded in compliant, documented process

Job To Be Done: Structure every deal correctly and protect gross.

Best Fit (ICP for Enrich):
- Dealers seeking improved profitability and efficiency at the desk
- Dealers who have NOT previously leveraged soft pulls in their sales process
- Natural next step for dealers currently on Protect OR on the retired Compliance-Only product
- Dealers wanting first-pencil discipline and consistency

Positioning Cautions:
- It is DIFFICULT to move existing Total Solution or Control customers DOWN to Enrich, because they will lose visibility into the score/report on the soft pull. Only attempt this downsell when cost pressure is severe.
- Enrich does NOT include soft pulls. If a dealer asks about soft pulls or pre-qualification workflows, position to Elevate or Control instead.

Value Message:
"Compliant deals, faster. Protect the dealership and profit the desk."

Persona Hooks:
- CEO/Dealer Principal: Desk efficiency + compliance in one investment
- CFO: Better PVR with no variable cost surprise
- GSM: Deals desked faster with payment visibility from the start
- F&I Director: Payment presentations integrated, STIPs handled proactively
- Compliance Officer: Same as Protect plus deal desking aligned to compliance workflow

chunks.title: PROTECT Tier — Detailed Definition
chunks.content:
PROTECT — Stop Fraud and Enforce Compliance

Job To Be Done: Eliminate fraud and enforce compliance.

What's Included:
- Hard pull credit reports
- Credit-Based Compliance Suite: Risk-Based Pricing Notices (RBPN), OFAC, Adverse Action letters, Red Flags compliance, Out-of-Wallet questions, Synthetic Identity protection
- Red Flags Hard Stop (only solution in the industry that prevents a credit report from being generated until all red flags are cleared)
- Fraud Prevention: Driver's License scan with instant fraud alerts
- Variable Ops Compliance Platform: paperless sales process, digital deal jacket creation, e-signature capabilities, enforceable compliant workflow, 5-year dead deal storage, internal fraud prevention, FTC-required physical & administrative compliance
- Enforceable workflows with process stops and real-time violation notifications

What's NOT Included: Soft Pulls are NOT in Protect. Customers needing soft-pull workflows should be positioned to Elevate or Control.

Best Fit (ICP for Protect):
- Dealers focused on preventing ID, synthetic, document, and internal fraud
- Dealers wanting to proactively enforce a streamlined, compliant sales process
- Dealers NEW to compliance and fraud automation
- Dealers with strong desk managers (so they don't need SmartPencil)
- Dealers happy with their current credit spend (don't need spend controls)

Value Message:
"Stop risk before it costs you. Stay protected, stay compliant, stay in business."

Persona Hooks:
- CEO/Dealer Principal: Risk protection, defensible process, liability reduction
- CFO: Compliance cost containment, predictable flat-rate pricing
- GSM: Faster customer intake, no fraud surprises late in deal
- F&I Director: Digital deal jacket, eSign, compliance enforcement in F&I
- Compliance Officer: Audit-ready documentation, Red Flags hard stops, OFAC automated

Never confuse Protect with the retired "Detect" tier — they are different and Detect no longer exists.

chunks.title: Informativ Product Tier Model — Current State (April 2026)
chunks.content:
CANONICAL TIER MODEL (effective April 2026) — supersedes all earlier tier definitions.

Informativ sells four product tiers. Each tier solves a different operational problem for the dealer:

| Tier      | Job To Be Done                                              | Key Differentiator                                |
|-----------|-------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
| PROTECT   | Stop fraud and enforce compliance                            | Hard pulls + Compliance Suite. NO soft pulls.     |
| ENRICH    | Structure better deals and protect profit                    | Adds SmartPencil deal desking to Protect.         |
| ELEVATE   | Reduce compliance burden + control credit spend              | Adds Soft-Pull-powered workflows + spend controls.|
| CONTROL   | Close faster with full credit access + guardrails            | Adds Full Hard Pull access on top of Elevate.     |

HIERARCHY (low → high): PROTECT → ENRICH → ELEVATE → CONTROL.

Each tier is additive: ENRICH includes everything in PROTECT plus SmartPencil. ELEVATE includes everything in ENRICH plus soft-pull prioritization and credit spend controls. CONTROL includes everything in ELEVATE plus full hard-pull access.

RETIRED TIERS (do not reference): Foundation and Detect were retired by October 2025. Do not position these tiers to customers or describe them as current product offerings. If a customer asks about Foundation or Detect, explain the product line was reorganized and reposition to Protect (entry tier) or Enrich (if they want deal desking).

PRICING NOTE: All tiers are flat-rate. Internal pricing details are NOT to be quoted to customers — reference the Internal Pricing Calculator or RSM only.

SmartPencil Sales Demo Outline
Product: SmartPencil
Company: Informativ
Audience: Dealer Principals, GMs, GSMs, Desk Managers, F&I
Purpose: Guide the buyer through the power of SmartPencil while uncovering pain points and aligning the tool to dealership needs
Estimated Demo Time: 20–30 minutes
Demo Flow Overview
1. Introduction & Framing (2–3 Minutes)
Talking Points:
Introduce yourself and Informativ
Frame the problem:
“The desk is where deals are won or lost. SmartPencil helps structure the right deal faster—with precision, consistency, and compliance built in.”
Discovery Questions:
How are deals structured today from first pencil to lender submission?
Where do deals slow down the most?
Who runs the desk—and is it consistent across your team?
Sales Goal:
Position SmartPencil as a performance and process upgrade — not a workflow disruption.
2. What is SmartPencil? (2 Minutes)
Talking Points:
SmartPencil is a credit-qualified desking tool that lives inside Informativ.
Instantly combines:
Customer credit profile
Vehicle/VIN data
Dealer-specific lender programs, markups, and rules
“It’s more than a calculator — it's a decisioning engine that surfaces the best deal, based on your rules and the customer’s credit.”
Discovery Questions:
Are you using a desking tool today? If so, what do you like/dislike about it?
Are your current payments lender-qualified or estimates?
How often are deals reworked after being submitted to lenders?
FAB:
3. Customer Onboarding & Pre-Qual (2–3 Minutes)
Demo Flow:
Scan license
Auto-fill customer info
Launch soft pull
Select vehicle by stock/VIN
Show pre-qual result (e.g. 721 score)
Talking Points:
“Sales reps can start the deal in seconds—with accuracy.”
“All relevant customer and vehicle data flows into the deal automatically.”
Discovery Questions:
When do you typically run pre-quals in the sales process?
Is this handled by sales, desk, or finance?
How often is data entry a pain point?
FAB:
4. Credit-Qualified Deal Structure (3–4 Minutes)
Demo Flow:
Navigate to SmartPencil tab
View loan vs. lease comparison
Show dynamic payment rendering
Talking Points:
“These are not generic payments — they’re built on your real lender programs.”
“Lease and loan options side-by-side help improve conversion and affordability conversations.”
Discovery Questions:
Are your sales team and desk managers presenting both lease and loan today?
How confident is your team in structuring deals based on lender criteria?
Do you ever lose gross by starting with the wrong payment?
FAB:
5. Deal Customization (3–4 Minutes)
Demo Flow:
Adjust down payment (flat or %)
Enter trade-in values
Show incentive grid (guaranteed + conditional)
Add F&I products, accessories, fees
Display TTL breakdown based on ZIP
Talking Points:
“Everything updates instantly—no need to rework the deal every time you change an input.”
“This keeps your team focused on closing, not calculating.”
Discovery Questions:
How often are incentives or fees missed in the deal structure?
Is TTL and tax calculation manual?
When are add-ons and F&I products introduced in your process?
FAB:
6. Profit Optimization & Guardrails (3–4 Minutes)
Demo Flow:
Show profit summary and line-item breakdown
Open reserve & flat fee modal
Show guardrails: LTV warnings, markup caps
Talking Points:
“The system tells you where your highest profit lives.”
“Guardrails prevent you from submitting deals that banks won’t approve — without locking you out.”
Discovery Questions:
Do your desk managers always know which lender pays the most?
Are there issues with deals getting kicked back from banks?
Do you have markups or flat fee strategies in place?
FAB:
7. Recommended Vehicles (2–3 Minutes)
Demo Flow:
Show SmartPencil recommending more profitable or affordable vehicles
Filter for aged inventory, lease programs, or alternatives
One-click to apply deal structure to new vehicle
Talking Points:
“If the first car doesn’t work, you don’t have to start over.”
“This turns SmartPencil into a selling tool—not just a desking tool.”
Discovery Questions:
How often do deals fall apart over payment or vehicle fit?
Do you use second pencil vehicles in the current process?
Is aged inventory a problem you’re trying to solve?
FAB:
8. Compliance, Deal Export, and Handoff (2–3 Minutes)
Demo Flow:
Save deal to Digital Deal Jacket
Push to Dealertrack or RouteOne
Show configurable print options (hide score, APR, lender)
Talking Points:
“You’re not just saving time—you’re capturing a full, compliant record.”
“The handoff to F&I is seamless—no more rekeying deals.”
Discovery Questions:
How is deal info passed to finance today?
How often do errors delay funding or compliance checks?
How important is printable, auditable documentation?
FAB:
9. Recap & Value Alignment (2 Minutes)
Talking Points:
SmartPencil gives you:
Faster deals
More profit
Better deal consistency
Fewer reworks
Stronger team performance
Discovery Questions:
What part of this would make the biggest impact on your store?
What do you see as the next step in getting this into your team’s hands?
10. Call to Action (1–2 Minutes)
Talking Points:
“Let’s configure SmartPencil to your lenders, rules, and process.”
“We can get you up and running quickly—this is built to fit your dealership, not the other way around.”
Closing Questions:
Would you like to move forward with a custom configuration?
Who else should be involved in an onboarding session?
Can we schedule a follow-up to kick off implementation?

Section | Purpose
1. Introduction & Framing | Set the stage and establish rapport
2. What is SmartPencil? | Define the tool and introduce value
3. Customer Onboarding | Show how the process begins
4. Deal Structuring | Walk through payment generation
5. Deal Customization | Show flexibility of the tool
6. Profit Optimization | Highlight financial impact
7. Vehicle Recommendations | Show deal-saving intelligence
8. Compliance & Deal Export | Close the loop
9. Recap & Value | Reinforce outcomes
10. Call to Action | Define next steps

Feature | Advantage | Benefit
Credit-qualified payments | Real lender logic used | Fewer reworks, more approvals


Feature | Advantage | Benefit
License scan + soft pull | Auto-populates customer record | Faster start, fewer errors
VIN-level vehicle match | Uses real inventory and pricing | Removes guesswork


Feature | Advantage | Benefit
Real-time lease/loan options | Based on customer credit and your lender feeds | Matches affordability, drives profit


Feature | Advantage | Benefit
Incentive and fee logic | Dealer-specific rules | Ensures accurate, compliant deals
F&I and accessories support | Fully baked into payment structure | Increases deal value


Feature | Advantage | Benefit
Reserve engine + guardrails | Highlights highest-profit structure | Improves front-end and back-end gross
Lender rule enforcement | Flags violations before submission | Reduces rejected deals


Feature | Advantage | Benefit
Recommended Vehicles | Real-time vehicle alternatives | Saves deals, moves aged inventory


Feature | Advantage | Benefit
Deal Jacket + LMS Export | Tracks full deal history | Supports compliance and speed
Custom print views | Dealer-branded worksheets | Control customer-facing experience

--- Slide 1 ---
Desking Manager



--- Slide 2 ---
New Domain:
Dealer Livelihood
It's all about the money.
Dealers are demanding solutions that reduce compliance exposure while improving financial outcomes.



--- Slide 3 ---
Desking Managers
are essential for profitability, efficiency, compliance and customer satisfaction.
May also be known as:
General Sales Manager
New Car Manager
Used Car Manager
Sales Manager



--- Slide 4 ---
Desking Managers are the Linchpin to the Money
Payment calculation​
Matching customers with lenders​
Approving or adjusting appraisals​
Using incentives & rebates​
Lender compliance​
Presentation of payment



--- Slide 5 ---
Road to the Sale



--- Slide 6 ---
Deal Building Reality: It's a Complicated Ecosystem
Deal Building Tools



--- Slide 7 ---
Creating a Desking Discipline
Gross profit steadily erodes
Desk consistency disappears
F&I inherits poisoned deals
Culture becomes reactive
GM spends days firefighting
Gross profit stabilizes predictably
Sales Managers execute with precision
F&I performance becomes reliable
Culture shifts to proactive
GM can focus on leadership
When it's
Weak
When it's
Strong



--- Slide 8 ---
Discipline at the Desk Delivers Results
65-75%
of Gross Profit
18-32%
Uplift in front end gross
30%
MoM drop
in PVR volatility when GSM coaches
Desking Strategy​
compared to peers when the GSM leads Desking Discipline
is influenced prior to the consumer visiting the showroom



--- Slide 9 ---
SmartPencil Delivers Discipline



--- Slide 10 ---
The Desking Lingo Game



--- Slide 11 ---
The Desking Lingo Game
Break into Groups (4 teams)
Pick a Team Captain
As a team, you will be asked to define key terms
No use of technology to assist
You have 30 seconds to raise your hand and read your teams response
Judges will Grade Your Answer (1-3)
We will add your points up to see who wins!
The winning team gets a prize!



--- Slide 12 ---
Example: Pencil



--- Slide 13 ---
Pencil: A deal worksheet showing different payment scenarios presented to the customer during negotiations



--- Slide 14 ---
Down Payment vs.Max Out of Pocket



--- Slide 15 ---
Down payment: The buyer’s initial cash contribution toward the total amount dueIncludes: Typically covers tax, title, license (TTL), and dealer fees — unless those are financed.Max out-of-pocket: Definition: Total cash due at signing (loan or lease), which is typically the max amount of money the consumer can contribute.
May not include:  first payment, TTL, doc, or dealer fees.



--- Slide 16 ---
Cap Costvs.Cap Cost Reduction



--- Slide 17 ---
Cap cost: The total amount being financed in a lease.Cap Cost Reduction: Any upfront payment or credit applied to lower the total cap cost.



--- Slide 18 ---
Front-End Grossvs.Back-End Gross



--- Slide 19 ---
Front-end gross:Gross profit from the vehicle sale itself.
Formula: Selling Price – Invoice – Trade Adjustments.
Back-end gross: Profit from F&I activities such as reserve and product sales.



--- Slide 20 ---
Interest Ratevs.Money Factor



--- Slide 21 ---
Interest Rate: Definition: The annual percentage rate charged by a lender for borrowing money.
Used For: Retail finance (loans).
Money Factor: The decimal representation of a lease’s interest rate. (and can be converted to APR by multiplying by 2,400)



--- Slide 22 ---
Buy Ratevs.Sell Rate



--- Slide 23 ---
Buy rate: the lender’s base rate offered to the dealerSell rate: the rate the dealer offers the customer (often marked up for profit)



--- Slide 24 ---
Flatvs.Super Flat



--- Slide 25 ---
Flat:A flat amount of money offered from a lender for loan or lease booking (ie: $500)Super flat: A variable amount of money offered from a lender for loan or lease contract (ie: 2.5% of amount financed)



--- Slide 26 ---
Reserve or Mark-upvs.Split



--- Slide 27 ---
Reserve / Mark-up: Dealer profit from an interest rate or money factor being marked up.Split: Profit-sharing agreement between dealer and lender, often for reserve or backend profit (e.g., 70/30).



--- Slide 28 ---
Residual Valuevs.Bookout Value



--- Slide 29 ---
Residual Value: The estimated value of a leased vehicle at the end of its term.  This is used in a lease calculation.Bookout Value: The market or lender-recognized value of a vehicle, used for loan approval and LTV calculations.



--- Slide 30 ---
Loan-to-Value (LTV)vs. Max Advance



--- Slide 31 ---
Loan-to-Value (aka: LTV): The percentage of vehicle value being financed.  Lenders usually set a max percentage(ex: 120%)Max Advance: The maximum financing amount a lender allows  based on credit tier and collateral. (ex: the dollar value of the LTV)



--- Slide 32 ---
Debt-to-Income (DTI) vs.Payment-to-Income (PTI)



--- Slide 33 ---
DTI: Debt to income ratio.  Used in credit considerations and financing approvals.PTI: Payment to income ratio.  Used in credit considerations and financing approvals.



--- Slide 34 ---
Guaranteed Incentivevs.Conditional Incentive



--- Slide 35 ---
Guaranteed Incentive:
Money provided by the OEM (sometimes the Lender) that is guaranteed consumer cash towards the deal (ex: $2000 consumer cash)
Conditional Incentives:
Money provided by the OEM (Sometimes the Lender) that is conditional upon the consumer qualifying for the incentive (ex: loyalty programs, private offers, etc)



--- Slide 36 ---
AND THE WINNER IS!?



--- Slide 37 ---
Selling Informativ SmartPencil
Positioning, Strategy & Sales Execution



--- Slide 38 ---
Why SmartPencil Exists
The Market Reality
Today's car shoppers think in payments, not price. They research online, compare options, and expect accurate monthly payments before ever contacting a dealer.
The problem? Dealers don’t have the tools to quickly enable or respond to car shoppers who are shopping by payment..
75%
Want Exact Payment
Shoppers expect monthly payment before dealer contact

chunks.content:
--- Slide 39 ---
Shoppers Think in Payments
Online Shopping
Research vehicles and compare options
Payment Question
Monthly payment is the first filter, not price
Dealer Contact
Accuracy builds trust and drives engagement
When dealers can't provide accurate, fundable payments immediately, shoppers lose confidence and move on to competitors who can.



--- Slide 40 ---
What SmartPencil Does
Informativ SmartPencil is a built-in feature within Informativ Compliance that instantly calculates credit-qualified lease and loan payments for all lenders, enabling transaction-ready, compliant, accurate payments from the first pencil.
Accurate From Start
Credit-qualified payments based on real credit tier, VIN, and jurisdiction
Compliant & Fundable
Every payment is transaction-ready and meets lender requirements
Faster Pencils
Auto-calculates all lender scenarios in seconds, not minutes



--- Slide 41 ---
Today's Agenda
01
Conversation Starters
Discovery questions and pain signals to open the SmartPencil conversation
02
SmartPencil Value Drivers
Speed, profit protection, and consumer trust outcomes
03
Positioning Within Tiers
How SmartPencil amplifies every Informativ tier
04
How to Close It
Walkthrough and next steps



--- Slide 42 ---
Conversation Openers
Use these discovery questions to uncover SmartPencil opportunities:
Payment Accuracy
"How confident are your desk managers in the accuracy of their first pencil?"
Speed to Quote
"How long does it take to calculate lease and loan payments across all your lenders?"
Fundability
"How often do deals get kicked back because the payment wasn't fundable?"
Desk Friction
"What's the biggest bottleneck between first pencil and F&I submission?"
Lender Back-and-Forth
"How much time does your team spend recalculating payments after lender feedback?"



--- Slide 43 ---
Pain Signals to Listen For
Operational Pains
Desk managers spending 10+ minutes per pencil
Constant lender calls to verify rates and terms
Deals stalling between desk and F&I
Inconsistent payment quotes across team
Recalculations eating up time
Customer Pains
Shoppers walking due to payment surprises
Trust erosion from inaccurate quotes
Extended wait times frustrating buyers
Deals falling apart at funding
Negative reviews citing payment issues



--- Slide 44 ---
SmartPencil Value Drivers
Speed & Efficiency
Instant credit-qualified payments eliminate manual calculations and lender back-and-forth
Profit Protection
Optimized structures maximize profit within compliance while reducing costly errors
Consumer Trust & Fundability
Accurate, fundable payments from first pencil build buyer confidence and close deals faster



--- Slide 45 ---
Precision, Performance & Profit
Credit-Qualified Payments
Real lease and loan calculations based on actual credit tier—not estimates
VIN-Level Precision
Exact vehicle data, incentives, and jurisdiction rules baked into every payment
Unlock All Profit Options
System presents best payment with profit-optimal structure automatically
"Turn your B & C players into A players." SmartPencil makes every desk manager an expert by automating complex calculations.



--- Slide 46 ---
The Outcomes That Matter
1
Faster Deals
Faster first pencil → faster decisions → faster closings. Eliminate wasted time on recalculations.
2
Protected Profit
Optimized structures and fewer errors mean more profit per deal with less risk.
3
Built Trust
Real, fundable payments from the start build consumer confidence and close more deals.



--- Slide 47 ---
Credit-Qualified ≠ Estimate
Traditional Payment Estimates
Generic calculations without credit data
Rough approximations that change later
Require manual adjustments and verification
Often not fundable as quoted
SmartPencil Credit-Qualified
Real lease/loan based on actual credit tier
VIN + credit tier + jurisdiction precision
Transaction-ready from first pencil
Fundable payments that close deals
The difference? SmartPencil delivers payments you can present with confidence—because they're built on real data, not guesswork.



--- Slide 48 ---
What Makes SmartPencil Accurate
Inventory & Pricing
Real-time VIN data and dealer cost
Lenders & Rates
All lender programs, markups, and incentives
Tax & Rule Logic
Jurisdiction-specific tax and compliance rules
Credit Tier Data
Actual credit tier from soft pull
Automated Engine
System handles complexity automatically
The engine combines all these inputs instantly to deliver credit-qualified, fundable payments—no manual work required.



--- Slide 49 ---
How SmartPencil Fits in Informativ
Compliance Base
Core compliance engine
SmartPencil Built-In
Instant credit-qualified payments across all lenders—no additional login
Supports a Digital Showroom
SmartPencil feeds accurate payments to every customer touchpoint
Key Message: SmartPencil amplifies every tier by ensuring payment accuracy flows through your entire operation.



--- Slide 50 ---
How SmartPencil Fits in Informativ Tiers



--- Slide 51 ---
Built Into Compliance
No New Login or Workflow
SmartPencil lives inside Informativ Compliance—your team uses the same system they already know
Shared Desk & F&I Visibility
Real-time transparency between desk and F&I eliminates handoff friction and speeds deals
Full Activity Tracking
Every payment calculation is logged and compliant, protecting your dealership from audit risk



--- Slide 52 ---
Let's Close SmartPencil
Why Buy
Faster deals and higher close rates
Protected profit with fewer errors
Consumer trust drives repeat business
Why Informativ
Built into Compliance—no new workflow
Proven accuracy across thousands of dealers
Real-time desk and F&I visibility
Why Now
Shoppers demand payment accuracy today
Competition is already moving faster
Every delayed deal costs profit
Next Steps
1
SmartPencil assessment
2
Configuration review
3
Pilot and rollout

INFORMATIVE PAYMENTS DEMO CERTIFICATION PROCESS
Audience: Regional Sales Managers (RSMs) Only
Goal: Certify every RSM to confidently deliver a consistent, value-driven Informativ Payments demo using the approved structure, messaging, and objection handling.
Outcome: Pass/Fail certification tracked internally.
STEP 1: KENT RECORDS THE GOLD-STANDARD DEMO - Complete
Owner: Kent
Content: Full demo of Informativ Payments including:
Brief positioning statement
Product walkthrough
Key features (card vaulting, compliance, speed to funding)
Clear value propositions
Call to action
Deliverables:
Loom video link of Kent’s live demo
Click-through sales deck used during the presentation
STEP 2: DISTRIBUTE DEMO MATERIALS TO RSM TEAM – IN PROGRESS
Owner: Sales Leadership
Deliverables to be shared with team:
Link to Kent’s recorded demo
Click-through deck
Word tracks script (hosted on Engage or Google Doc)
Instructions to RSMs:
Watch the full demo
Review the deck and word tracks
Practice multiple times (self-recording or peer-to-peer sessions)
STEP 3: WORD TRACK PRACTICE
Resource: Sales script/word track doc
Expectations:
Internalize the sales flow and language
Practice responses to dealer objections
Optional: Roleplay with a peer for feedback
STEP 4: SCHEDULE CERTIFICATION DEMO
Action: Each RSM schedules a live mock demo session with:
Sales Director
Ask Chuck (optional observer/facilitator)
Kent
Format: Zoom or in-person
Time Allotted: 30 minutes
Scenario: Deliver the demo as if presenting to a prospective dealer, including common objection handling and a close
STEP 5: DEMO DELIVERY AND EVALUATION
Live Evaluation Includes:
Full walkthrough of Informativ Payments
Real-time feedback on presentation style and content
Soft objections introduced by leadership during the roleplay
STEP 6: SCORECARD + FEEDBACK – SCORECARD COMPLETE
Certification Result: Pass or Fail
Tracking: Certification outcomes stored in a shared tracker or CRM custom field
RSMs who do not pass will receive targeted coaching and must re-certify within 3 business days
CERTIFICATION SCORECARD (LIVE DEMO)
Final Result: Pass / Fail
Evaluator: [Name]
Date: [MM/DD/YYYY]
FEEDBACK LOOP
Post-certification, note and collect:
Common dealer objections
Gaps in understanding or delivery
Suggestions for updating word tracks or demos
Sales Enablement and Kent will use this feedback to refine demo materials over time.

Category | Criteria | Pass/Fail | Notes

Product Knowledge | Clearly explained features and functionality |  | 
Value Proposition | Clearly communicated value to the dealer |  | 
Word Tracks | Used approved transitions and language |  |

Delivery | Presentation was clear, professional, and engaging |  | 
Close | Delivered a compelling CTA or next step |  |

chunks.content:
SMART PENCIL – USER CHEAT SHEET (Updated
Modules 1–6)
What Smart Pencil Needs to Work
- Digital Deal Jacket
- Valid ZIP Code
- Vehicle of Interest (VIN)
- Privacy Authorization
- Pre■Qualification (or Smart Pencil Consent on Enrich)
These elements must be present before accurate payments can be generated.
Top Screen Basics
- Save Deal: stores changes to the deal
- Print: sends worksheet to Digital Deal Jacket
- GSM Arrow: shows profit breakdown
- Loan vs Lease toggle changes all calculations
Loan Buttonology
- Term selections (48/60/72/84 etc.) are dealership■defined
- System highlights BEST payment for customer and dealer
- Captive Lender Only option limits to OEM programs
- Incentives refresh automatically from JD Power
- Add■Ons allow F&I; products to be included in payment
- Max Out of Pocket vs Pay All Fees Up Front controls cash due
Lease Buttonology
- Months and mileage grids are preconfigured by dealer
- Money Factor and Residual drive lease math
- System calculates first payment + fees due at signing
- Taxes and fees adjust by state automatically
- Profit arrow shows front■end and back■end profit on leases
Recommendations Rail
- Use when original vehicle becomes unavailable
- Sort by: Best Payment, Price, Profit, Days on Lot, etc.- Build New Deal keeps trade and payoff data
- Eliminates rebuilding a deal from scratch
Push to LMS / RouteOne
- Use ‘Send Update Lead to RouteOne’ to export deal
- Deal structure becomes locked from Smart Pencil side
- Save Deal must be clicked before pushing
- Prevents manual re■entry in lender systems
Quick Quote – Core Concepts
- When enabled, system defaults to Quick Quote first
- Standardizes every first pencil at the dealership
- Generates loan and lease estimates using dealer rules
- Still requires VIN, ZIP, Privacy, and Prequal for accuracy
- Clicking Build Deal converts Quick Quote into full Smart Pencil
- After Save Deal, the system no longer defaults back to Quick Quote
- Printing from Quick Quote saves a worksheet to the deal jacket
Best Practices for Salespeople
- Start with Quick Quote to present an initial payment
- Move to Build Deal for real negotiations
- Negotiate TERM before price to preserve profit
- Use Add■Ons for F&I; products rather than changing vehicle price
Best Practices for CSAs
- Confirm dealership settings during setup
- Verify fees and incentives are correct
- Ensure Quick Quote rules match store process
- Train users on Save Deal vs Print differences

Smart Pencil Dealership Playbook
How your team uses Smart Pencil day-to-day (quick quote, build, print, and lender push)
Audience: Sales, Desk Managers, F&I, GM (Dealer-facing)  •  v1.0  •  February 13, 2026
Placeholder note: Replace bracketed items like [INSERT SCREENSHOT] or [OWNER] before publishing.

## What Smart Pencil Needs (Do This First)

Digital Deal Jacket created for the customer
Valid customer ZIP code (required for accurate taxes/fees/rebates)
Privacy authorization signed
Prequal OR SmartPencil Consent (depends on your package)
Vehicle of interest added (VIN)

## The SmartPencil Workflow (Most Common)

Start the deal with Quick Quote (if enabled at your store).
Review the initial loan/lease payments (your store’s defaults).
Click Build Deal to open the full Smart Pencil desk worksheet.
Adjust term, cash down, trade, incentives, and add-ons as needed.
Click Save Deal to keep your configuration for the next time you open it.
Click Print when you are ready to show the customer (this stores the worksheet in the deal jacket).
Send Update Lead to RouteOne/Dealertrack to move to the deal forward to the LMS.

## Quick Quote (Your Standardized First Pencil)

If Quick Quote is enabled, SmartPencil defaults here first.
Payments are based on your store’s configured terms and assumptions.
Quick Quote is for speed and consistency; Build Deal is for full Specific SmartPencil desking.
[INSERT SCREENSHOT: Quick Quote]

## Build Deal vs Save Deal vs Print


## Recommendations (When the Vehicle Changes or Sells)

Open the Recommendations rail to view alternate vehicles in your inventory.
Sort by Best Payment, Profit, Days on Lot, Price, and more.
Click Build New Deal to pivot vehicles while keeping trade/payoff information.
[INSERT SCREENSHOT: Recommendations rail]

## Pushing to RouteOne/Dealertrack (LMS)

Use Send Update Lead to RouteOne to export the deal structure.
If you change something in Smart Pencil, click Save Deal first, then send again.
This reduces duplicate work and keeps the deal consistent across systems.
[INSERT SCREENSHOT: Send Update Lead to RouteOne]

## Best Practices


## Support and Reinforcement

Week 1: usage check + fixes
Week 2–4: workflow reinforcement (save/print/push)
Week 8 & 12: adoption review with leadership
[INSERT CONTACT: Support channel / escalation path]

## Quick Definitions

Digital Deal Jacket (DDJ): the customer’s document folder; Smart Pencil printouts are stored here.
Quick Quote: your store’s standardized first pencil (if enabled).
Build Deal: opens the full Smart Pencil desk worksheet.
Save Deal: keeps your changes in the Smart Pencil view.
Print: saves a worksheet copy into the DDJ.
LMS Push: sending the deal to RouteOne/Dealertrack so you don’t rebuild it.

Action | What it does | When to use it
Build Deal | Creates the full Smart Pencil worksheet | After Quick Quote when you need real deal structure
Save Deal | Saves your current configuration for later | Any time you change numbers and want it to stick
Print | Stores the worksheet in the Digital Deal Jacket | When you are presenting to the customer / need audit trail


Role | Do this | Avoid this
Sales | Start in Mobile App; scan VIN if able, use Vehicle Information Tile in Digital Showroom (Sales.Dsgss.com) | Skipping ZIP/VIN/privacy; rebuilding from scratch elsewhere
Desk Manager | Use term negotiation; use Recommendations to pivot; push to LMS | Manual re-entry in RouteOne when a push exists
F&I | Use add-ons/F&I products; keep print trail in DDJ | Showing a deal without printing/recording the worksheet
GM | Set the standard: Smart Pencil is the starting point | Letting multiple processes coexist indefinitely

### Path 4: New Customer (Virtual)

Prework required: logins verified; device/browser check; test customer built; VIN available.
Session 1 (60–75 min): Informativ foundation + Smart Pencil quick start.
Session 2 (45–60 min): manager deep dive (profit, incentives, add-ons, lease).
Certification: module quizzes 1–6 assigned to internal champions; optional dealer quiz subset.
Follow-up cadence: 1/2/3/4/8/12 weeks with adoption scorecard review.


## Live Deal Training Script (Desk‑Side)

Use consistent phrasing; it reduces confusion and makes training repeatable.


## Objection Handling (Quick Counters)



## Adoption Metrics and Scorecard (Even Without Analytics Access)

Use the provided metric categories; track manually until GA access is restored.


## Follow‑Up Cadence (Post‑Training)

Week 1: confirm logins + first builds; resolve blockers; reinforce Quick Quote rule.
Week 2: confirm Saved Deals and Printed Deals; check DDJ trail consistency.
Week 3: validate Recommendations use case; run a pivot drill.
Week 4: validate LMS push adoption; prevent rebuild behavior.
Week 8: review usage trend; address objections; refresh managers.
Week 12: confirm standardization; identify internal champion; set ongoing rhythm.


## Appendix A: Module Reference (Modules 1–6)

Module 1 – Getting a customer ready for Smart Pencil: DDJ, ZIP, privacy, prequal/consent, VIN; mobile vs showroom VIN entry rules.
Module 2 – Top-of-screen buttonology: home, customer details, documents/DDJ, Save Deal vs Print, profit/GSM arrow, fee toggles.
Module 3 – Loan controls: captive lenders, lender selection, incentives, add-ons, max out-of-pocket vs pay fees up front, print options.
Module 4 – Lease controls: term/mileage grids, money factor/residual, due-at-signing, lease-specific fees, incentives/add-ons.
Module 5 – Recommendations + LMS push: recommendations rail, sorting, Build New Deal keeps trade; Save Deal then Send Update Lead to RouteOne.
Module 6 – Quick Quote: default mode when enabled; standardized first pencil; Build Deal transitions to full Smart Pencil; Save Deal changes what you see next.


## Appendix B: Placeholders

[INSERT SCREENSHOT: Quick Quote screen]
[INSERT SCREENSHOT: Recommendations rail + Build New Deal]
[INSERT SCREENSHOT: Save Deal vs Print buttons]
[INSERT SCREENSHOT: Send Update Lead to RouteOne]
[INSERT LINK: Updated One‑Sheet Cheat Sheet PDF]
[INSERT LINK: Internal certification quiz CSVs Modules 1–6]

Role | Training Emphasis | Non‑negotiables
GM | Why + adoption expectations; Profit Visibility | Quick Quote standardization; managers accountable to usage
Desk / Sales Manager | Deal building, profit, incentives, term strategy | Build Deal + Save Deal + Print Deal; Recommendations; LMS push
F&I | Reserve/markups visibility, add-ons/F&I products | Print workflow; compliance trail; lender handoff
Sales | Fast workflow, scan → VIN→ SIGN | Correct inputs (ZIP/VIN/privacy); do not bypass steps
Internet/BDC (optional) | Lead-to-quote speed | Quick Quote behavior and notification of the desk


Pathway | Context | Delivery Shape | Primary Risk
1) Existing customer upgrading (onsite) | Already knows Informativ process | Manager meeting + floor refresh + desk training | Reverting to old desking tool / RouteOne rebuild habit
2) New customer (onsite) | Net new to Informativ + Smart Pencil | Layered: compliance foundation → Smart Pencil | Cognitive overload; skipping fundamentals
3) Existing customer (virtual) | Behavior change without onsite energy | Short sessions + enforced follow-ups | No accountability; adoption decays
4) New customer (virtual) | Hardest pathway | Prework required + live build + certification + cadence | Low engagement; poor configuration readiness


Moment | Trainer line (suggested) | Why it matters
Inputs | “No ZIP, no accuracy. No VIN, no payment.” | Reinforces required data discipline
Quick Quote | “Quick Quote is your standardized first pencil.” | Frames default behavior
Build Deal | “Now we’re building the real deal structure.” | Moves from estimate to full desk
Save Deal | “Save keeps our configuration for the next time we open it.” | Clarifies persistence
Print | “Print puts the worksheet in the deal jacket; that’s the paper trail.” | Compliance + audit trail
Recommendations | “Vehicle sold? Pivot here without rebuilding the tower.” | Teaches the power move
LMS Push | “Send to RouteOne so nobody rebuilds this deal twice.” | Protects workflow integrity


Objection | Counter (short) | Proof point / demo move
We already have a desking tool. | Smart Pencil builds once and pushes to LMS; your tool becomes the finish line. | Build → Save → Send to RouteOne in front of them
This slows me down. | Quick Quote standardizes the first pencil in seconds. | Open Quick Quote and print
I’ll just build it in RouteOne. | That’s building twice. Smart Pencil eliminates duplicate entry. | Show locked deal structure after push
Quick Quote isn’t how we pencil. | Quick Quote reflects YOUR parameters; it’s your worksheet digitized. | Review dealer-config terms/mileage/defaults


Category | Metric | Manual proxy (if needed)
Activity | Last Dealer Activity | Manager confirms last usage date; sample 5 users
Activity | # Users by Dealer / last activity by user | List active users; spot-check logins
Flow | # Unique Customers with Credit & Payments Generated | Count last 10 DDJs with printed worksheet
Flow | # Unique Customers with Smart Pencil Opened | Count smart pencil opens from saved/printed artifacts
Outcome | # Unique Customers with Saved Deals | Sample: saved deal exists for 10 customers
Outcome | # Unique Customers with Printed Deals | Count Smart Pencil docs in DDJ for last 10 deals
Outcome | # Unique Customers with Deals sent to LMS | RouteOne/Dealertrack received deals count
Downstream | Which deals move forward? | Manager estimate + compare funded deals sample

Purpose of Quick Quote
Why dealerships enable it
Default behavior when Quick Quote is on
What is still required to build an accurate deal

Question,Option A,Option B,Option C,Option D,Correct Answer
Quick Quote is designed to:,Replace the prequal process,Standardize how every first pencil starts,Submit deals directly to lenders,Remove taxes and fees,B
"True or False: When Quick Quote is enabled, Smart Pencil defaults to the Quick Quote screen first.",TRUE,FALSE,,,A
Which items are still required to generate an accurate Smart Pencil even when using Quick Quote?,"Digital Deal Jacket, ZIP code, Privacy, Prequal/Consent, and VIN",Only a customer name,Only a VIN number,None of the above,A
Why would a dealership choose to enable Quick Quote?,To hide payments from customers,To standardize the initial deal structure for all users,To remove lender options,To bypass credit checks,B
True or False: Quick Quote can build a preliminary payment even before a full deal is built.,TRUE,FALSE,,,A
What happens when the user clicks Build Deal from the Quick Quote screen?,The deal is printed automatically,The system creates a full Smart Pencil deal,The customer record is deleted,The Quick Quote resets to zero,B
"After a user clicks Save Deal inside Smart Pencil, what occurs the next time they open the deal?",It returns to Quick Quote mode,It displays the saved structured deal instead of Quick Quote,It deletes incentives,It locks the customer record,B
True or False: Printing from the Quick Quote screen saves a Smart Pencil worksheet to the Digital Deal Jacket.,TRUE,FALSE,,,A
Quick Quote payments are based on:,Random estimates,"The dealership’s predefined parameters and settings, and best for both parties.",Only MSRP pricing,Credit score only,B
True or False: Quick Quote can generate both loan and lease estimates if the vehicle qualifies.,TRUE,FALSE,,,A
Which action moves a Quick Quote deal into full Smart Pencil mode?,Clicking Save Deal,Clicking Build Deal,Printing the quote,Adding incentives,B
"If Quick Quote is not enabled by the dealership, what will happen?","When the pieces are in place, The system will create a Smart Pencil",The customer cannot be created,Leases will not appear,RouteOne push will be disabled,A
True or False: Quick Quote is intended to speed up the initial desking process for salespeople and managers.,TRUE,FALSE,,,A
What determines the default payments shown in Quick Quote?,Customer credit score only,The dealership implementation settings,User preference,VIN number only,B
True or False: A vehicle of interest (VIN) must still be added for Quick Quote to produce payment information.,TRUE,FALSE,,,A

Question,Option A,Option B,Option C,Option D,Correct Answer
In Smart Pencil clicking the Loan tab displays:,Lease payment options,Only trade information,Loan payment grids and options,Customer details,C
True or False: Switching from Loan to Lease changes the payment calculation shown at the bottom of the screen.,TRUE,FALSE,,,A
Lease options will typically only appear when:,A used vehicle is selected,A new vehicle that qualifies for leasing is selected,The user clicks Save Deal,Captive lenders are turned off,B
What is a captive lender?,Any local credit union,The OEM financing arm such as GM Financial for Chevrolet,A subprime bank,A third party lender,B
True or False: The Captive Lenders Only toggle limits payment options to the manufacturer financing programs.,TRUE,FALSE,,,A
When the Show All Lenders option is selected the system will:,Display every configured lender option,Only show the best lender,Hide captive lenders,Reset the deal,A
True or False: Dealers can add their own lenders such as a local credit union as long as rate sheets are provided.,TRUE,FALSE,,,A
Smart Pencil encourages negotiation on which element instead of price or trade?,Vehicle color,Interest rate,Term,Add ons,C
Why is negotiating on term preferred over negotiating on price?,It hides fees,It keeps dealership profit intact while adjusting payment,It removes taxes,It requires no approvals,B
The green Best tag represents:,The lowest interest rate,The option that best balances customer payment and dealer profit,The longest term,The highest rebate,B
The profit breakdown panel shows which two main categories of profit?,Taxes and fees,Front end profit and back end profit,MSRP and retail,Loan and lease,B
True or False: Adjusting the invoice price will directly change the displayed dealer profit.,TRUE,FALSE,,,A
Reserve Split refers to:,Splitting profit between salespeople,Splitting finance profit between the dealership and the bank,Splitting trade value,Splitting taxes,B
Which of the following is considered BACK END profit?,Vehicle selling price,Trade allowance,F&I products like warranty or GAP,Retail price,C
Incentive programs in Smart Pencil are refreshed from data providers approximately:,Once per day,Every two hours,Once per week,Only at the beginning of the month,B
True or False: Users can manually add custom incentives in addition to system loaded incentives.,TRUE,FALSE,,,A
Custom Incentives in Smart Pencil can also be treated as:,Add ons,Discounts,Taxes,Fees,B
The Add Ons section is used for:,Adjusting interest rates,Adding optional products like wheels tires or packages,Changing customer information,Updating VIN numbers,B
True or False: Add ons can be preconfigured to appear on every deal by the implementations team.,TRUE,FALSE,,,A
The Pay All Fees Up Front toggle controls:,Whether taxes and fees are included in the monthly payment,Whether the deal can be printed,Whether lenders are shown,Whether incentives are applied,A
Turning ON Pay All Fees Up Front will generally result in:,Lower monthly payment and higher due at signing/out of pocket.,Higher monthly payment and lower due at signing,No change to payments,Removal of taxes,A
Which field represents what the customer still owes on their trade?,Trade Value,Trade Allowance,Trade Payoff,Invoice,C
Which field represents what the dealership is actually offering the customer for their trade?,Trade Payoff,Trade Allowance,Trade Value,Retail Price,B
True or False: Updating trade payoff value or allowance will immediately recalculate profit and payment.,TRUE,FALSE,,,A
Taxes titling and licensing amounts in Smart Pencil are generated by:,Manual entry,"A tax calculation API, Dealer provided fees ",The CRM system,The lender,B
True or False: Each Smart Pencil deal requires a Zip Code to ensure accurate Tax and Rebate Information.,TRUE,FALSE,,,A
Clicking Save Deal in the Loan screen will:,Add the worksheet to the Digital Deal Jacket,Save current changes for later viewing,Print the deal,Reset all fields,B
Clicking Print Deal will:,Save the worksheet into the Digital Deal Jacket,Submit the deal to lenders,Delete the current configuration,Only create a paper copy,A

Go here, send to route one.

And once we're in route one, we can do what we need to do. We can adjust the deal as we need to. So what that's going to do is just you guys know if you've been training this platform for a while when these dealers get to.
To this point, we've always had a conversation. I say always, always as a promise. I always had this conversation where, oh, I've got to rebuild this deal in route one. Well, now they don't have to. # Informativ Payments – Talking Points

“Speed Up Deals. Get Paid Faster. Protect Profit.”


## What Informativ Payments Does

Informativ Payments is a new built-in feature within Informativ Compliance that instantly calculates credit-qualified lease and loan payments for all lenders.
This helps dealers present transaction-ready, compliant, accurate payments from the start.
Key value messages to use:
Instantly turns desk managers into “A-Players”
Auto-calculates all lender scenarios in seconds
Eliminates back-and-forth with lenders
Brings consistency and accuracy to every pencil
Speeds up deals
Faster first pencil → faster desk decisions → faster closings
Reduces time wasted on recalculations or incorrect quotes
Protects dealership profit
Optimizes payment structures automatically
Reduces errors that cost the dealership money
Helps ensure the deal a desk manager quotes is immediately fundable
Builds consumer trust
Payments are credit-qualified, accurate, compliant
Customers see a real payment—not a guess
Minimizes rehashing, customer frustration, and lost confidence
Built directly into Informativ Compliance
No new login
No new workflows
Desk & F&I get real-time visibility
All activity is tracked for compliance transparency


# Launch & Timing Talking Points (Important)



## Why Payments Is NOT Launching in December

Dealers are in their busiest month of the year.  Per Informativ’s culture of “Trust, Respect & Do the Right Thing,” we do not release new system updates during December to avoid any risk—no matter how small—of performance interruptions.
Suggested Line: “December is your highest-volume, highest-pressure month. We do not push new features during this time because we won’t risk even a 1% chance of slowdown or disruption. We’re protecting your business.”


## January Installations

We are building a priority list for January installations.
Suggested Line: “Dealers already on the priority list will get Payments installed first—January schedules will fill up fast.”


## Webinar & Demo Talking Points



### December 10th Webinar

Exclusive to Informativ clients
Live demo of Informativ Payments
Walkthrough of real payment-speeding and profit-protection scenarios
Q&A with product leadership
Helps staff understand the rollout and value before January installs
Suggested line: “If customers want to see how it works before January, the webinar on December 10th is the perfect opportunity.”


### Offer a 1:1 Custom Demo

If they want their own walkthrough:
Suggested Line: “We’re happy to set up a personal demo for your dealership or your entire group. We’ll walk your desk, sales, and F&I teams through exactly how Payments fits your workflow.”
SmartPencil DEMO


# Smart Pencil Demo Script (v0 Prototype)



## 0. Quick Setup / Opening

[On Zoom or in-person, after intros]
Rep:
Thanks again for taking the time today, [Prospect Name].
What I’d like to do is:
1. Give you a little background and context on why Smart Pencil exists and how it fits into the Informativ ecosystem.
2. Walk through the core workflow in the app using our v0 prototype, so you can see exactly how your team would use it day to day.
3. Then we can talk about roadmap items and any specific questions you have around digital retail, tablets, permissions, etc.
Does that sound good?


## 1. Background & Why Smart Pencil Exists

[Slide: “Background / Why we built this”]
Rep:
About four years ago, my co-founder and I started a company called CarMatic, and we partnered with dealers to build technology that modernizes automotive payments.
Coming out of COVID, we saw a very specific problem:
- A lot of consumers were shopping by payment and by feature set,
- Some websites could support that,
- But your BDCs and sales teams didn’t have tools that brought that world together in the showroom.
So we built CarMatic as essentially an inventory-driven payment engine:
- You take your inventory,
- Filter it by key parameters,
- And we’re especially good at monthly payment and key feature sets,
- Then, once you land on the right car, you desk the deal out right there.
As part of that desking capability, we built a payment engine that can:
- Take a large amount of data from all your lenders,
- Run thousands of different payment scenarios in the background,
- So you always see the best options for the customer
- While also maximizing your profitability.
What Informativ did was take that engine and marry it with real credit data and compliance.
So now:
- We match a real credit profile against your actual lender programs,
- We do that in a way that traditional desking tools simply haven’t done,
- And the result is what you’ll see today: Smart Pencil, powered by Informativ’s credit and compliance plus CarMatic’s payment tech.
You’re getting an early look at what this solution looks like as a v0, and I’ll show you both how it works today and what’s coming next.


## 2. What Problem Smart Pencil Solves

[Slide: “Consumers want it. Dealers need it.”]
Rep:
We’ve done the research with consumers and dealers, and a few big themes keep coming up.
On the consumer side:
- Shoppers want payment scenarios upfront,
- They want to know what they can actually afford,
- They want to know which vehicles fit their budget.
The pricing conversation has really shifted from “What’s the price?” to “What’s the payment?”
I’m sure you’re seeing that in your own showrooms.
On the dealer side, the feedback is:
- A lot of tools in the market are based on estimates
- Hard-coded rates
- Captive-only programs
- Or outdated assumptions.
- They’re often not VIN-specific,
- They don’t tie tightly enough to real lender rules and credit profiles.
Smart Pencil solves a few key things:
1. It gives you credit-qualified payments the moment you have credit insight.
2. It eliminates the swivel-chair back and forth between separate credit and desking systems.
3. It’s VIN-level specific and built to elevate profit on every structure.

## 3. Concept: Credit-Qualified Payments

[Slide: “Credit Qualified Payments”]
Rep:
When we bring this to life in your store, we call it credit-qualified payments.
The instant you get credit insights — whether via a soft pull or another pre-qual workflow — Smart Pencil:
- Automatically generates real payments in the system,
- Based on the actual VIN and the customer’s real pre-qual data,
- No more, “Work the credit over here, then flip back to your desk over there.”
You’ll see:
- It’s VIN-level specific,
- It seeks out the best payment for the consumer,
- While optimizing profit based on your rules and your lenders.
One piece of feedback we hear consistently from dealers is:
“Not all of my desking managers or sales managers are A-players… But this helps them get there.”
Smart Pencil is really about:
- Turning your B and C players into A-players,
- Giving everyone a best-in-class desk inside the Informativ platform.


## 4. Where Smart Pencil Lives (Informativ Ecosystem)

[Slide: “Inside the Informativ Ecosystem”]
Rep:
Smart Pencil is not a separate system with separate logins.
It lives inside the Informativ ecosystem you already use:
- Same platform you use for credit pulling,
- Same compliance center,
- Same customer dashboards,
- Just a new tab and new views that appear in that workflow.
A few key points:
- You keep accuracy and compliance at the core,
- We measure performance uplift and speed/efficiency,
- And we always optimize for profit according to your deal structures and your rules.
No new logins. No new system to learn. It just becomes part of how your team runs a deal.


## 5. Configuration: Making Smart Pencil Match Your Store

[Slide: “Configuration & Onboarding”]
Rep:
When we onboard [Store Name], we walk you through a set of configuration questions so the system behaves exactly how you want to operate.
Things like:
- Which lenders do you want to include?
- How do you want deals structured? (e.g., down payment defaults, payoff/fees rules)
- What are your rate markup rules?
- What are your fee rules?
- How do you want to handle lease vs. loan on first pencil?
Once that’s set:
- Smart Pencil will deliver instant first pencils on lease and loan,
- Using what we call our “Best Payment” logic.
“Best Payment” means:
- Lowest possible consumer monthly payment,
- While maximizing your profit within your rules and lender constraints.
We’ll jump into what that looks like in the app now.


## 6. High-Level Process Flow

[Slide: “Process Flow in the Showroom”]
Rep:
Let’s do a quick walk-through of where Smart Pencil fits in your sales process.
Imagine:
- You have a customer in the showroom,
- They’re ready to go on a test drive.
Today, if you’re maximizing the Informativ platform, you:
1. Scan the driver’s license in the Informativ app.
2. That populates your pre-qual submission.
3. With Smart Pencil, you then select the VIN or stock number of the vehicle they’re about to drive.
4. The system automatically does a soft pull.
5. The second that soft pull returns, payment scenarios are generated.
From there you can:
- Open the pencil,
- Save it,
- Modify it,
- Print it,
- Or present it digitally — depending on how your sales process runs.
Our goal is to help you accelerate to “yes”:
- Get to a real pencil quickly,
- With real lender logic,
- And then move to hard pull, submit to lenders, get stips, and close.


## 7. Live Demo Walk-Through (v0 App)

[Switch to the app prototype / screen share]
7.1 Scanning the Driver’s License & Selecting the Car
Rep:
Let’s pick up that scenario in the app.
[In the Informativ mobile or desktop app]
We’ll start by:
1. Scanning the customer’s driver’s license using the existing Informativ app.
- That pre-populates your pre-qual.
- We leverage the buyer’s location — zip code and address — because that’s critical to the lending equation.
2. Next, we select the vehicle:
- You can search by stock number or VIN,
- Choose the car they’re test-driving today.
We’ll VIN-decode that down, and we’ll leverage:
- The VIN,
- The customer’s zip code,
- And your configured lender programs.
7.2 Submitting the Pre-Qual (Existing Informativ View)
[Click “Submit Pre-Qual” in the app / show the existing screen]
Rep:
Now we submit that pre-qual app.
You’re already familiar with this:
- You get signatures and consent,
- You submit,
- The pre-qual score comes back into the Informativ Compliance Center.
[Show the Compliance Center view]
You’ll notice in our new UI:
- You still have your pre-qual score column,
- But now you’ll see two new columns: Loan and Lease.
These values:
- Are specific to this consumer’s profile,
- For the exact VIN we selected,
- And they’re generated using our Best Payment logic.
So right from this view, you already know:
- “Here’s what we can do on a loan,”
- “Here’s what we can do on a lease,”
- Based on real lender programs, not just guesses.
7.3 Opening Smart Pencil
[Click into the loan/lease payment to open Smart Pencil tab]
Rep:
When we click into that payment, we’re introduced to Smart Pencil.
Notice:
- It’s just a new tab inside the Informativ platform.
- You’re not bouncing out to a separate desking system or logging into anything new.
Another benefit:
- When you decide to present a particular pencil, Smart Pencil can add that document into your Document Center.
- It becomes part of your digital deal jacket — printable, storable, and compliant.

## 8. Smart Pencil Screen Walk-Through

8.1 Vehicle & Price
[Point to inventory / vehicle info at top]
Rep:
At the top, we integrate with your inventory:
- All your inventory feeds into Smart Pencil,
- We pull any pricing associated with that VIN.
You can:
- Adjust the price at any time (for example, discounting or changing selling price),
- And Smart Pencil will re-run the scenarios underneath.
8.2 Deal Details (Down Payment, Trade, Fees)
[Point to “Deal Details” section]
Rep:
Here in Deal Details, we configure how you want to start each pencil.
In this example:
- We’ve started with a $2,000 down payment.
But during onboarding, you can set:
- “We always start first pencil at $3,500 down,” or
- “We want to set down as a percentage of the amount financed.”
You can override it on any given deal.
This particular structure also has:
- A trade value,
- A trade payoff,
- So you can see how equity or negative equity flows into the deal.
The structure you see here is:
- Payoff and fees up front:
- Down payment
- Tax, title, and license
- Dealer fees
- For a lease, that will also include the first month’s payment if you choose.
All of that is configurable to match exactly how you like to structure your deals.
8.3 Best Loan & Best Lease
[Point to Loan on left, Lease on right]
Rep:
On the left, you’ll see our Best Loan.
On the right, our Best Lease.
We’re comparing multiple scenarios here:
- Different terms — for example, 48, 60, 72 months
- For leases, different mileage options as well.
The system is:
- Running through all your lender and term combinations in real time,
- And identifying the best scenarios for this customer:
- The lowest payment for them,
- While maximizing profitability for you.
8.4 Lender Grid: Rates, LTV, Payment, Profit
[Scroll to/show the payment grid]
Rep:
Here’s where Smart Pencil really shines — the payment grid.
For each lender scenario, you can see:
- Which lender it is
- The rate
- LTV
- Any cash due
- Monthly payment
- Total cost
- And your profit scenarios (front and back)
This grid is:
- Fully aligned with your lender rules,
- And it takes into account:
- Lender-specific markup rules,
- Super flats vs. markups,
- Lenders that allow no markup, etc.
Example:
- If your default configuration says “mark up all loans and leases by 2%,”
- But a particular lender only allows 1%, or only offers a flat,
- Smart Pencil respects that and optimizes around it.
We’ve walked into stores where managers say, “We always take the flat,” and we show them that:
- In some cases, they’re leaving money on the table,
- Because another structure or lender combo yields higher profit at the same or similar payment.
Smart Pencil does those calculations for you in real time.


## 9. Quick Quote / Standardized First Rate (Optional Script)

[Only cover this if they talk about standardized first pencil / disparate impact]
Rep:
Some dealer groups we work with show everyone the same rate on the first pencil, regardless of credit, to manage fairness and disparate impact concerns.
They’ll say, “Everyone sees 10.9% on the first pass,” for example.
We built a mode for that called Quick Quote.
With Quick Quote:
- You can run a standardized first pencil — even before pulling credit if needed,
- Or after credit is pulled, you can still choose to present that standardized rate on the first pencil,
- Then, once you’re ready, you can move into the optimized Smart Pencil scenarios.
In practice, it’s as simple as:
- Overriding the rates on the first pencil (e.g., 11%, 12%, 13%),
- Hitting print or presenting digitally,
- And the system can still keep track of the final best-case Smart Pencil scenario in the background.


## 10. Incentives, Add-Ons, and TTL

10.1 Incentives
[Scroll down to incentives section]
Rep:
Smart Pencil automatically includes:
- Any guaranteed incentives you qualify for,
- And we handle stackability and combinability properly.
You can:
- Add conditional incentives (targeted, private offers, etc.) with a quick click,
- And the system automatically calculates the maximum cash and best payment scenario.
If you have:
- Unique or temporary programs,
- Or a “manager discount” you want to show,
You can add that as a custom incentive in this section as well.
10.2 Add-Ons
[Scroll to add-ons section]
Rep:
Down here we have Add-Ons.
During configuration, you can set:
- Static products you want to pre-load on every deal —
e.g., a $2,200 care package that includes maintenance, tire warranty, etc.
We can default these on or off.
You can also:
- Add VIN-specific add-ons (accessories, special programs) on the fly,
- Use this section to capture F&I products you want included in the payment.
Just type or select the product and Smart Pencil will fold it into the deal instantly.
10.3 Tax, Title, and License
[Scroll to TTL section]
Rep:
At the bottom, we have a jurisdiction-specific tax, title, and license engine.
Because we already know:
- The buyer’s address / zip code,
We can calculate TTL for that jurisdiction and:
- Itemize everything for you:
- State and local sales tax,
- Title fee,
- License fee,
- Registration fee, etc.
It’s all broken out, so:
- Your pencil is accurate,
- And your team isn’t guessing on out-the-door numbers.

## 11. Profit View (“Profit Button”)

[Click to expand the profit details section]
Rep:
Up here, we’ve got what we affectionately call the Profit Button.
We keep it collapsed by default because:
- Some managers don’t want this visible in every scenario,
- But when you expand it, you get a full breakdown of profit:
You’ll see:
- Front-end and back-end grosses, itemized,
- Which structure is driving what profit,
- How markups vs. flats are impacting the deal.
For example:
- On a given loan, Smart Pencil might automatically select the super flat because it yields higher profit than a markup.
- If the super flat is 5% of the amount financed and that’s above your target profit, it chooses that.
- If a markup yields more profit within lender rules, it selects the markup instead.
At any time, a manager can:
- Override the system,
- Change a rate,
- Adjust a flat,
- Or tweak any of those profit levers.
The key is: Smart Pencil does the heavy lifting, so your managers and new desk guys aren’t trying to manually run hundreds of scenarios in their head.


## 12. Printing & Presenting to the Customer

12.1 Print-Out Options (Current v0)
[Click “Print” / show PDF options]
Rep:
Let’s talk about how you present this to the customer.
Smart Pencil has a very flexible print-out engine:
- You can choose to show or hide:
- Specific lenders,
- The interest rate,
- The credit score,
- Whether you show multiple options or just one.
If you want a fully loaded print-out:
- You can include all the details,
- It prints as a clean, branded PDF you can hand to the guest.
If you don’t want to show certain things — like:
- Interest rate,
- Specific lenders,
- Or internal numbers,
You simply toggle those fields off, and they disappear from the customer view.
12.2 Tablet / Digital Presentation (Permissions)
Rep:
You asked about digital pencils and tablets instead of printed ones — especially for luxury or Genesis-type experiences where OEMs don’t want printed pencils anymore.
Smart Pencil supports that vision in two ways:
1. Permission-based views
- Your desking managers can see full detail, including profit and lender grids.
- Your salespeople can have a limited view:
- No profit,
- No lender names,
- Just the payment and terms they need to present.
2. Device-agnostic UI
- This can absolutely be presented on a tablet/iPad as a clean digital pencil.
- The salesperson can walk the guest through payments without ever printing a four-square.
[Optional future-looking note, if you want to mention roadmap]
Looking slightly ahead on our roadmap:
- We’re adding the ability for salespeople to present the pencil in the app,
- Have the customer circle or tap the option they like,
- And initial/sign on the tablet,
- So that sign-off is stored as part of the digital deal jacket.
We’re also working on a geofence-based offer:
- You can present the offer in the showroom,
- It remains visible to the customer while they’re on your lot,
- If they leave, the offer expires and can’t be taken down the street and shopped.
That’s part of our near-term roadmap following this v0.


## 13. Future Enhancements & Recommendation Engine (Roadmap)

[Optional “What’s Coming” slide]
Rep:
Beyond what you see in this v0, there are a couple of enhancements that dealers have been especially excited about:
1. Remote presentation via text
- Send a pencil directly to a customer’s phone via SMS,
- They can review terms remotely,
- And you still control what they see.
2. In-app customer presentation view
- A salesperson view that’s strictly customer-friendly,
- With possible approval/initial capture built in.
3. Recommendation engine for alternative vehicles
- Let’s say a customer wants a specific vehicle (e.g., a Camry) and the numbers aren’t working.
- Smart Pencil will be able to run scenarios against your entire inventory and say:
- “Here are three similar vehicles that fit this customer’s affordability profile.”
- You can define:
- Whether it should prioritize higher profit alternatives,
- Same year/make/model,
- Or comparable but more affordable units (new vs. used, etc.).
4. Cash deals & one-pay lease scenarios
- We’re folding in a clean way to:
- Run cash pencils with TTL,
- And show lease payment options even when the customer is bringing a large cash amount,
- So you can still maximize reserve and profitability.


## 14. Integrations: RouteOne, Dealertrack, etc.

Rep:
We also want your workflow to be efficient end-to-end.
Once you land on a deal structure you like in Smart Pencil:
- You can push that deal into RouteOne or Dealertrack,
- And soon into CUDL as well.
So for you:
- There’s no re-keying of the structure,
- No manual typing of payments and terms into your finance portals,
- You just push it and continue your process.


## 15. Wrap-Up & Next Steps

[Stop screen share or go back to summary slide]
Rep:
So, to summarize what Smart Pencil does for [Store Name]:
- It gives you credit-qualified, VIN-specific payments instantly,
- Turns your average desk managers into A-players by doing the heavy optimization work,
- Keeps everything inside the Informativ ecosystem you already use,
- And gives you a clear, flexible way to present pencils digitally or on paper,
- All while maximizing front-end and back-end profit within your rules and lender constraints.
From what you’ve seen, does this align with what you were hoping for in a desking tool?
[Pause for feedback & questions]
Rep (if they’re interested in moving forward):
Perfect. The next step would be:
- We’ll schedule your configuration/onboarding session,
- Get your lenders, markups, and products dialed in,
- And then roll Smart Pencil out to your stores with training for your sales and desk teams.

# Informativ Payments – Talking Points

“Speed Up Deals. Get Paid Faster. Protect Profit.”

## What Informativ Payments Does

Informativ Payments is a new built-in feature within Informativ Compliance that instantly calculates credit-qualified lease and loan payments for all lenders.
This helps dealers present transaction-ready, compliant, accurate payments from the start.
Key value messages to use:
Instantly turns desk managers into “A-Players”
Auto-calculates all lender scenarios in seconds
Eliminates back-and-forth with lenders
Brings consistency and accuracy to every pencil
Speeds up deals
Faster first pencil → faster desk decisions → faster closings
Reduces time wasted on recalculations or incorrect quotes
Protects dealership profit
Optimizes payment structures automatically
Reduces errors that cost the dealership money
Helps ensure the deal a desk manager quotes is immediately fundable
Builds consumer trust
Payments are credit-qualified, accurate, compliant
Customers see a real payment—not a guess
Minimizes rehashing, customer frustration, and lost confidence
Built directly into Informativ Compliance
No new login
No new workflows
Desk & F&I get real-time visibility
All activity is tracked for compliance transparency

# Launch & Timing Talking Points (Important)


## Why Payments Is NOT Launching in December

Dealers are in their busiest month of the year.  Per Informativ’s culture of “Trust, Respect & Do the Right Thing,” we do not release new system updates during December to avoid any risk—no matter how small—of performance interruptions.
Suggested Line: “December is your highest-volume, highest-pressure month. We do not push new features during this time because we won’t risk even a 1% chance of slowdown or disruption. We’re protecting your business.”

## January Installations

We are building a priority list for January installations.
Suggested Line: “Dealers already on the priority list will get Payments installed first—January schedules will fill up fast.”

## Webinar & Demo Talking Points


### December 10th Webinar

Exclusive to Informativ clients
Live demo of Informativ Payments
Walkthrough of real payment-speeding and profit-protection scenarios
Q&A with product leadership
Helps staff understand the rollout and value before January installs
Suggested line: “If customers want to see how it works before January, the webinar on December 10th is the perfect opportunity.”

### Offer a 1:1 Custom Demo

If they want their own walkthrough:
Suggested Line: “We’re happy to set up a personal demo for your dealership or your entire group. We’ll walk your desk, sales, and F&I teams through exactly how Payments fits your workflow.”

TurboPass Dashboard Training
*All content on this document along with additional information can be found at: https://knowledgebase.turbopassreport.com/en/knowledge-base
How to Videos
How to use the TurboPass Dashboard
Sending reports through the dashboard
Consumer Journey Walk Thru
What the end user sees when completing a TurboPass invitation
Dashboard Features
CODE - A unique code used to identify TurboPass invitations/reports from one another
STATUS - Indicates the current/most recent step in the customers invitation process. The "i" button provides the definition of the current status.
REQUESTOR - The TurboPass user who sent the customer the TurboPass invitation
INVITATION - Indicates where the TurboPass invitation was sent from
REBUILD REPORT - Refreshes the TurboPass report with the most recent transactions to date within 30 days of the initial generation date. This feature is also helpful regenerating reports that may have had errors or missing information. NOTE: If the customer changes their log in within this time, this feature is unavailable.
SHARE (ARROW BUTTON) - This button allows the TurboPass user to easily send the TurboPass report to your lender. This feature is only limited to lender email domains and can not be sent to any other domains such as gmail, outlook, yahoo, etc.
COMPLETE IN THIS BROWSER - Gives the TurboPass user the ability to have the customer complete the invitation process on their dashboard
RESEND INVITE - This feature allows a TurboPass user to resend the same TurboPass invitation to the customer. This button is mostly used to remind the customer to complete the TurboPass invitation without having to send a new TurboPass.
SEND NEW TURBOPASS - This feature allows the TurboPass user to send a new TurboPass invitation to a customer without having to reenter the customer's information. Once you click on this button it will take you back to the invitation information and direct you to insert the Last 4 digits of the customer's SSN or Pin code again. Once completed, click 'Create'.
HISTORY - This feature displays the chronological order of steps the customer has taken in the invitation process. This also provides the Technical Status which can provide more information on specific errors than the friendly status shown on the dashboard.
INSTITUTION SEARCH - This feature provides a search for institutions that are available to connect to by each reports data provider. It is important to note that institution statuses vary and may not be available to connect to even if displayed on search.
