chunks.source: Credit Customer Detailed Verified STIPS Battle Cards
chunks.filename: Credit Customer Detailed Verified STIPS Battle Cards.docx
chunks.content:
Verified Stips Battle Card – Credit-Only Customers
(Not on Informativ Compliance — Using Informativ Credit Only)
Primary Positioning
Verified Stips powered by TurboPass verifies income, residence, and employment instantly, pulling data directly from the source – and it plugs right into RouteOne and DealerTrack — no need for our Compliance platform.
Top 5 ROI Hooks
Prevent Fraud – Verified data direct from source, no chance of falsified docs.
Speed Decisioning & Funding – From days to minutes, improves cash flow.
Modern Customer Experience – No scanning, emailing, or uploads for the customer.
Lender Origination Fee Rebates – Some lenders rebate fees when stips are pre-verified.
No Workflow Change – Fits into RouteOne/DealerTrack just like current stips.
Key SDR Talk Track
“You’re already using Informativ Credit. Verified Stips is a plug-in that lets you send fully verified stips right to RouteOne or DealerTrack — no change to your process, just faster funding, fewer fraud risks, and in some cases, lender rebates.”
Common Objections & Rebuttals
Objection: “We already collect stips directly from customers.”
Rebuttal: “That’s exactly where fraud slips in. Verified Stips pulls data directly from the source, so you don’t have to rely on customer-provided documents.”
Objection: “We don’t want to change our process.”
Rebuttal: “No change needed — your team keeps using RouteOne or DealerTrack the same way, the only difference is the stips arrive pre-verified.”
Closing Question
“If you could prevent fake docs and fund faster without changing your process, is there a reason you wouldn’t look at this?”
Fraud Prevention & Risk Mitigation Hooks
If someone provides a fake pay stub today and it slips past your current process, how much could that cost you in bad paper, repo expenses, and lost time?
You already verify credit profiles—why not add verified income and insurance checks to stop fraud before the deal ever reaches funding?
How confident are you that your current stip review process would catch a high-quality digital forgery before it leaves your lot?
Operational Efficiency & Compliance Hooks
4. If your team could collect and verify stip documents electronically—no personal emails, no photo texts—how much faster and cleaner would your deal jackets be?
5. Fraudsters tend to hit when you’re busiest. How valuable would it be to have a verification step that runs instantly, even on your peak sales days?
6. When a lender asks for proof you verified income or insurance, how valuable would it be to hand over a timestamped, third-party report instead of a stack of paper copies?
Multi-Layered Fraud Approach Positioning
You’re already protecting the credit side of your deals. Adding Verified STIP covers the other major fraud vector—falsified stips—giving you protection from multiple angles. Which point of entry do you think fraudsters will target next?
In today’s market, do you feel safer relying on a single fraud check, or building a layered system that shuts down bad deals at every step?

chunks.source: TS or Compliance Customer Detailed Verified STIPS Battle Cards
chunks.filename: TS or Compliance Customer Detailed Verified STIPS Battle Cards.docx
chunks.content:
Verified Stips Battle Card – Compliance Customers
(On Informativ Compliance or Total Solution)
Primary Positioning
Verified Stips powered by TurboPass is an upgrade to your current secure stip collection process. It automates the verification step — pulling income, residence, and employment data directly from the source in real time.
Top 5 ROI Hooks
Upgrade Existing Security – Already secure, now fully automated verification.
Prevent Fraud – Stops falsified documents before they reach the desk.
Speed Funding – Cuts stip collection from days to minutes, accelerates lender funding.
Lender Rebates – Some lenders waive or rebate origination fees for pre-verified stips.
No Process Change – Uses your existing Informativ Compliance workflow.
Key SDR Talk Track
“You’re already ahead with secure stip collection in Informativ Compliance. Verified Stips is the next step — it eliminates customer uploads entirely by pulling verified data from the source, so deals fund faster and fraud risk drops to near zero.”
Common Objections & Rebuttals
Objection: “We already have a secure system for stips.”
Rebuttal: “Absolutely — Verified Stips builds on that by removing the need for the customer to send any documents at all. It’s about speed and fraud-proofing, not replacing what works.”
Objection: “I don’t want to retrain my team.”
Rebuttal: “No retraining — it’s built into your current workflow, just an added step that runs in the background.”
Closing Question
“Since you’re already secure, why not take the next step and make it instant?”
Fraud Prevention & Risk Mitigation Hooks
If a fraudster slips through your current process today, what would it cost you—in lost inventory, chargebacks, or legal exposure?
We already catch fake IDs and run Red Flags. Wouldn’t it make sense to verify stip documents with the same level of certainty before you fund the deal?
How confident are you that your current stip collection process would detect a digitally altered pay stub or insurance card before delivery?
Operational Efficiency & Compliance Hooks
If your team could collect and verify all stip documents digitally—no texting, no Gmail—how much time and risk would that remove from each deal?
We know the busiest days are when fraudsters strike. What would it mean to have an automated verification step that works just as fast on Saturday at 5 PM as it does on Tuesday morning?
When the FTC or your lender asks how you verified income or insurance, how valuable would it be to pull up a timestamped, third-party validation instead of a photocopy?
Multi-Layered Fraud Approach Positioning
You’ve already got ID verification and compliance checks through Total Solution. Adding Verified STIP means we’re protecting your deals at every point—ID, credit, and now stip verification. Which layer do you think fraudsters will try to break through first?
With fraud on the rise and schemes getting more sophisticated, do you feel safer relying on a single check—or a layered system that shuts the door at every step?

External Threat / Regulation​ | Dealer Pain / Risk​ | Informativ Solution​ | Value Delivered​
FTC Safeguards Rule​ | Risk of $51,744 per violation due to missing ID verification, unsecured PII, or lack of audit trail​ | Informativ’s Compliance Suite, Digital Deal Jackets​ | Automates compliance steps, secures PII, provides audit logs & manager oversight​
FTC CARS Rule​ | Deceptive practices, unclear disclosures → regulatory penalties​ | Digital Credit Apps + Prequal via Customer Insights​ | Enforces transparency & tracks buyer consent/documentation​
Auto Loan Fraud Surge​ | Synthetic identities, fake documents → unrecoverable vehicle losses​ | Fraud Suite: ID Scan + Barcode Check, SIM/Device Verification, Soft Pull Fraud Insights​ | Stops fake identities before the deal, validates buyer authenticity​
CDK Data Breach​ | Operational shutdown, lost consumer data trust​ | Secure Document Collection Portal, PII Encryption & User Controls​ | Protects dealership from ransomware risk & enforces employee data handling security​
State-Level Privacy Laws​ | Exposure to consumer lawsuits over mishandled data​ | Automated Privacy Notice, Risk-Based Pricing & Adverse Action Fulfillment​ | Proves consumer disclosure compliance and reduces liability​
Title Washing & VIN Scams​ | Accepting falsified vehicle titles or buyer info​ | DL Scan Validation + AI Matching​ | Verifies documents + flags inconsistencies for desking managers​
Dead Deal Documentation Issues​ | Lost revenue, failed audits, no re-marketing value​ | Digital Dead Deal Jacket Storage + Reporting​ | Revives opportunities, improves compliance posture​
Consumer Trust & Reputation Damage​ | Bad reviews, chargebacks, and lender risk​ | CreditDriver Soft Pull with Consent Flow​ | Builds trust by offering consumer-first, score-safe prequal experience​

Features, Advantages, and Benefits of Informativ
1. TotalSolution Platform
Feature: Unified platform for credit, compliance, ID verification, and lead engagement.
Advantage: Streamlines processes by reducing the number of vendors and logins.
Benefit: Saves time and money while improving operational efficiency across F&I, Sales, and Compliance teams.
2. Credit Report Integration (Experian, Equifax, TransUnion)
Feature: Pull hard and soft credit reports from major bureaus.
Advantage: Full flexibility depending on buyer engagement stage.
Benefit: Improve conversion with soft pulls during lead gen, and streamline funding with reliable hard pull accuracy.
3. Compliance Automation (Dealer Safeguard)
Feature: Automated compliance workflows (Red Flags, OFAC, Adverse Action, Privacy Notices).
Advantage: Ensures regulatory alignment without manual tracking.
Benefit: Reduces legal risk and audit exposure while protecting your dealership’s reputation.
4. Digital Deal Jacket Export for DMS Integration
Feature: Drag and drop deal jackets directly to DMS (2-step process).
Advantage: Ensures critical documents are securely and consistently archived.
Benefit: Simplifies compliance documentation and supports faster funding and auditing processes.
5. Mobile Driver’s License Scanning
Feature: Scan and verify IDs from any mobile device.
Advantage: Speeds up customer intake and ensures accurate data capture.
Benefit: Reduces fraud risk and enhances customer satisfaction during the initial interaction.
6. Website Credit Application Integration
Feature: Plug-and-play soft pull tools and credit applications for your dealership website.
Advantage: Instant consumer pre-qualification online.
Benefit: Increases lead quality and showroom traffic with pre-qualified buyers.
7. QR Code Lead Capture + Sales Enablement
Feature: Generate QR codes for vehicle listings, marketing, or on-lot displays.
Advantage: Creates an easy entry point for buyers and links directly to pre-qualification tools.
Benefit: Boosts lead capture and enables quicker buyer engagement, even after hours.
8. Real-Time Deal Alerts + Compliance Tracking Dashboard
Feature: Centralized dashboard to monitor deal activity and compliance flags.
Advantage: Gives managers live oversight of all open deals and alerts.
Benefit: Prevents deal delays and enhances accountability across the team.
9. Multi-Channel Support & Dedicated Account Management
Feature: White-glove onboarding and dealer-first support team.
Advantage: Personalized service and fast resolution times.
Benefit: Minimizes downtime and ensures a smooth transition from other providers.

Recommended Tier: CONTROL

CONTROL delivers complete showroom oversight and advanced tools to minimize friction, manage costs, and empower high-performance teams.
Key Product Benefits
Showroom Credit Spend Controls: Optimize usage across rooftops with alerts and guardrails
Customer Insights Dashboard: Boost closing rates by identifying buyer potential early
Compliance Enforcements: Real-time fraud alerts, Red Flags hard stops, enforced workflows
Soft Pull Integration: Reduce compliance burden while increasing engagement
Enterprise Scalability: Multi-rooftop dashboard, custom forms, and robust audit support
Value Messaging

Objection Handling Prompts
Competitive Differentiation

Objection | Response
“We already have a fraud solution.” | Most systems stop at detection. Informativ CONTROL automates enforcement with real-time process stops and showroom credit optimization.
“Compliance isn’t a major issue right now.” | FTC requirements have changed—CONTROL ensures you’re protected today and audit-ready tomorrow.
“It’s too much for our group.” | CONTROL is modular and configurable—roll out to high-risk rooftops first and scale as needed.


Informativ CONTROL | 700Credit/NCC
Real-time enforced workflows | Manual alert systems
Multi-rooftop dashboard visibility | Rooftop-level view only
Integrated soft pull strategy | Often requires third-party tools
Fully bundled with CBC credit pricing | À la carte add-ons drive up cost

Features, Advantages, and Benefits of Informativ
1. TotalSolution Platform
Feature: Unified platform for credit, compliance, ID verification, and lead engagement.
Advantage: Streamlines processes by reducing the number of vendors and logins.
Benefit: Saves time and money while improving operational efficiency across F&I, Sales, and Compliance teams.
2. Credit Report Integration (Experian, Equifax, TransUnion)
Feature: Pull hard and soft credit reports from major bureaus.
Advantage: Full flexibility depending on buyer engagement stage.
Benefit: Improve conversion with soft pulls during lead gen, and streamline funding with reliable hard pull accuracy.
3. Compliance Automation (Dealer Safeguard)
Feature: Automated compliance workflows (Red Flags, OFAC, Adverse Action, Privacy Notices).
Advantage: Ensures regulatory alignment without manual tracking.
Benefit: Reduces legal risk and audit exposure while protecting your dealership’s reputation.
4. Digital Deal Jacket Export for DMS Integration
Feature: Drag and drop deal jackets directly to DMS (2-step process).
Advantage: Ensures critical documents are securely and consistently archived.
Benefit: Simplifies compliance documentation and supports faster funding and auditing processes.
5. Mobile Driver’s License Scanning
Feature: Scan and verify IDs from any mobile device.
Advantage: Speeds up customer intake and ensures accurate data capture.
Benefit: Reduces fraud risk and enhances customer satisfaction during the initial interaction.
6. Website Credit Application Integration
Feature: Plug-and-play soft pull tools and credit applications for your dealership website.
Advantage: Instant consumer pre-qualification online.
Benefit: Increases lead quality and showroom traffic with pre-qualified buyers.
7. QR Code Lead Capture + Sales Enablement
Feature: Generate QR codes for vehicle listings, marketing, or on-lot displays.
Advantage: Creates an easy entry point for buyers and links directly to pre-qualification tools.
Benefit: Boosts lead capture and enables quicker buyer engagement, even after hours.
8. Real-Time Deal Alerts + Compliance Tracking Dashboard
Feature: Centralized dashboard to monitor deal activity and compliance flags.
Advantage: Gives managers live oversight of all open deals and alerts.
Benefit: Prevents deal delays and enhances accountability across the team.
9. Multi-Channel Support & Dedicated Account Management
Feature: White-glove onboarding and dealer-first support team.
Advantage: Personalized service and fast resolution times.
Benefit: Minimizes downtime and ensures a smooth transition from other providers.

Prospecting
Objection: New product/upgrade to TS will bring changes to internal processes.
Rebuttal:
Totally fair — but the goal isn’t to add more process, it’s to remove the most time-wasting, repetitive steps. Informativ automates compliance, reduces rework, and makes the process consistent across reps. We replace manual friction with guided, faster workflows.
Objection: Compliance doesn’t matter now that the CARS rule is dead.
Rebuttal:
CARS rule aside, the FTC Safeguards Rule is very much alive — and it’s enforceable. Beyond regulation, compliance is how you protect your business from lawsuits, chargebacks, and public exposure.
Objection: We’ve never experienced fraud.
Rebuttal:
That’s great — but fraud is growing fast. Synthetic IDs, mobile-first scams, and social engineering are harder to detect. Informativ puts controls in place before you become a target.
Objection: Not adding any new expenses. (Prospecting/Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
What if we could lower your overall cost by replacing 2–3 separate vendors with one? Informativ isn’t just a new expense — it’s a smarter investment with real ROI.
Objection: Too many logins and systems already. (Prospecting/Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
Informativ was designed to reduce system creep. One login, one guided workflow — no toggling between platforms or chasing down paperwork.
Discovery
Objection: I want the flexibility to run as many credit pulls as I want.
Rebuttal:
That flexibility comes at a cost — literally. Most dealers overspend on unnecessary pulls that don’t fund. We help you qualify customers smarter, reduce cost per deal, and still give you override options when needed.
Objection: I don’t want to change my process.
Rebuttal:
You don’t have to overhaul everything — but if the current process is costing you time, money, or risk exposure, isn’t it worth seeing if there’s a better way? We’ve helped teams streamline what they already do without disrupting their flow.
Objection: Not all stores operate the same, need setup flexibility.
Rebuttal:
No problem — our platform scales to each store’s structure. You can standardize where it helps (compliance, fraud prevention) while customizing access, workflows, and reporting by location.
Objection: Your system doesn’t fit my process.
Rebuttal:
That’s exactly what we solve for. We adapt to your current workflow and eliminate steps that
slow you down. Informativ replaces fragmented, outdated systems with an integrated, dealer-designed experience.
Objection: I can’t get my team to use the tools we already have. (Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
That’s common — most tools were built for IT, not the sales desk. Informativ is intuitive, fast, and designed for real-world use. We also support your launch with training and reinforcement to drive adoption.
Objection: We’re happy with our current credit provider.
Rebuttal:
Understood. But are they helping you protect against fraud? Ensure compliance? Improve desk speed? We go beyond credit pulls — we protect the entire deal from lead to funded file.
Objection: We already have digital deal jackets. (Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
Perfect. Then Informativ becomes the upgrade — with compliance triggers, ID checks, and real-time fraud alerts layered into your deal jacket for added protection.
Objection: We already have compliance and fraud tools. (Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
That’s a good start — but are they connected? Informativ ensures your compliance, fraud, and credit workflows are integrated and enforceable — not operating in silos.
Objection: Vantage score doesn’t work for us. (Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
That’s why we offer mirror-image soft pulls — same tradeline data, same FICO score ranges as a hard pull. You get actionable insights without the credit hit.
Objection: We have a provider that offers the same services. (Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
Many claim that — few deliver the full solution. Informativ is credit, fraud, and compliance in one unified platform — with real-time protections and full audit trails. Let’s compare side-by-side.

TD
Objection: I need a different hard pull for my credit unions.
Rebuttal:
We get that — and our platform supports the ability to segment pulls based on lender requirements. Informativ is designed to work with your funding partners, not against them.
Objection: I’ve always done 3 hard pulls — that’s what my lenders accept. (TD/Pricing)
Rebuttal:
Totally makes sense, and we can support that workflow. But let’s talk about how many of those are actually needed to close the deal. Soft pull + intelligent hard pull strategy can cut cost and get lenders what they need — without unnecessary spend.
Objection: True enterprise access for showroom reps that hop between floors.
Rebuttal:
Informativ is built for flexibility. We support multi-user, multi-location access and permissions — ideal for roaming reps or sales floors that shift teams.
Objection: All forms need to be digitized, but there are limitations.
Rebuttal:
Our digital jacket supports full FTC compliance and key deal docs. Where OEM or DMS limitations exist, we integrate around them — so your team can still stay digital and compliant.
Objection: I need more than one hard pull within 30 days.
Rebuttal:
We support multi-pull strategies and can structure your workflow to accommodate lenders that require them — while still reducing pull volume and cost when possible.
Objection: This adds another step in the process. (Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
Only if you count eliminating paperwork, skipping manual checks, and preventing compliance gaps as a “step.” Informativ actually removes effort from your team by guiding them through a faster, protected workflow.
Objection: I can’t get my team to use the tools we already have. (Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
That’s common — most tools were built for IT, not the sales desk. Informativ is intuitive, fast, and designed for real-world use. We also support your launch with training and reinforcement to drive adoption.
Objection: Price and disrupting our current process.
Rebuttal:
We reduce cost and simplify process — not the other way around. Most dealers find we cut monthly tech costs and reduce effort at the same time.
Objection: We were told all documents would be digitized.
Rebuttal:
That’s the goal — and we deliver. Some documents may still need to originate from external systems, but Informativ brings them together in one secure, audit-ready digital jacket.
Objection: I need more than one hard pull for CUs.
Rebuttal:
And we support that. Informativ lets you configure how, when, and where pulls happen — so your credit union relationships stay intact without overspending on every deal.
Objection: We have a provider that offers the same services. (Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
Many claim that — few deliver the full solution. Informativ is credit, fraud, and compliance in one unified platform — with real-time protections and full audit trails. Let’s compare side-by-side.
Objection: We were sold on seamless integration — but we’ve seen issues.
Rebuttal:
That’s why we build native integrations — not surface-level ones. We’ll work with your current system to fix duplication, sync data correctly, and reduce friction.
Objection: Too many logins and systems already. (Prospecting/Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
Informativ was designed to reduce system creep. One login, one guided workflow — no toggling between platforms or chasing down paperwork.
Pricing
Objection: Too expensive.
Rebuttal:
Most of our dealers recover their investment in the first few months — whether by preventing one fraud case, reducing bureau spend, or improving lead-to-sale conversion. It’s not an expense — it’s a smarter way to operate.
Objection: You can’t compete with 700 Credit on price.
Rebuttal:
Price matters — but value matters more. Informativ offers not just credit but compliance, fraud protection, and sales enablement in one platform. 700 Credit is one slice. We’re the full solution.
Objection: I feel like I’m paying more and getting less. (TD/Pricing)
Rebuttal:
Let’s break it down. Are you paying for multiple disconnected tools that don’t talk to each other? We bundle it into one streamlined platform with more visibility, fewer steps, and stronger ROI.
Objection: Owner is a penny pincher.
Rebuttal:
Then they’ll love Informativ. Most of our clients actually cut spend by consolidating credit, fraud, and compliance tools into one platform. Let’s walk through what they’re paying today vs. our flat-rate model.
Objection: I’m a small dealer — this is more than I’m spending now.
Rebuttal:
Understandable. But what if we could protect you from one fraud case, or automate a compliance issue that could cost thousands? Informativ was built to scale — small stores benefit the most from smart automation and protection.
Objection: Just too expensive.
Rebuttal:
When you factor in bureau fees, fraud risk, compliance gaps, and wasted time — Informativ usually pays for itself in the first 60 days. We also offer tiered options that meet dealers where they are.

--- Slide 1 ---
External Pressures, Internal Solutions
External Threat / Regulation | Dealer Pain / Risk | Informativ Solution | Value Delivered
FTC Safeguards Rule | Risk of $51,744 per violation due to missing ID verification, unsecured PII, or lack of audit trail | Informativ’s Compliance Suite, Digital Deal Jackets | Automates compliance steps, secures PII, provides audit logs & manager oversight
FTC CARS Rule | Deceptive practices, unclear disclosures → regulatory penalties | Digital Credit Apps + Prequal via Customer Insights | Enforces transparency & tracks buyer consent/documentation
Auto Loan Fraud Surge | Synthetic identities, fake documents → unrecoverable vehicle losses | Fraud Suite: ID Scan + Barcode Check, SIM/Device Verification, Soft Pull Fraud Insights | Stops fake identities before the deal, validates buyer authenticity
CDK Data Breach | Operational shutdown, lost consumer data trust | Secure Document Collection Portal, PII Encryption & User Controls | Protects dealership from ransomware risk & enforces employee data handling security
State-Level Privacy Laws | Exposure to consumer lawsuits over mishandled data | Automated Privacy Notice, Risk-Based Pricing & Adverse Action Fulfillment | Proves consumer disclosure compliance and reduces liability
Title Washing & VIN Scams | Accepting falsified vehicle titles or buyer info | DL Scan Validation + AI Matching | Verifies documents + flags inconsistencies for desking managers
Dead Deal Documentation Issues | Lost revenue, failed audits, no re-marketing value | Digital Dead Deal Jacket Storage + Reporting | Revives opportunities, improves compliance posture
Consumer Trust & Reputation Damage | Bad reviews, chargebacks, and lender risk | CreditDriver Soft Pull with Consent Flow | Builds trust by offering consumer-first, score-safe prequal experience

chunks.source: Informativ Payments — Core Value Propositions and Talk Tracks
chunks.content:
Informativ Payments — Core Value Propositions and Talk Tracks

VALUE PROP 1 — Move from Estimated to Qualified Payments:
Most platforms still show "estimated" payments that fall apart in-store. Informativ delivers real, credit-qualified, transactable payments, calculated exactly the way a dealership desks a deal. This closes the expectation gap that kills trust and conversion.

VALUE PROP 2 — More Than Penny-Perfect… We Return the Best Payment:
Legacy systems produce penny-perfect math. Informativ optimizes for the best payment — one that blends incentives, lender programs, and dealer margin to produce lower payments for shoppers and higher gross for dealers. No one else aligns both sides.

VALUE PROP 3 — Built for Software Companies, Not Legacy Dealer Tools:
Where competitors retrofitted old desking systems into APIs, Informativ Payments is cloud-native from day one. Low latency, modern architecture, and clean documentation make integration fast and future-proof for product teams.

VALUE PROP 4 — Usage-Based Pricing Dealers Actually Love:
Partners only pay for the VINs they push payments to, not a blanket rooftop fee. This makes payment experiences more affordable to dealers and more flexible to package inside partner platforms.

VALUE PROP 5 — Compliance as a Core Capability:
Powered by Informativ's credit, identity, and compliance backbone used by thousands of dealers. Every payment meets lender, OEM, and regulatory requirements. Partners get an enterprise-ready layer of safety without added engineering or legal overhead.

VALUE PROP 6 — Payments Everywhere (Ads, Websites, Chat, CRM, Digital Retailing):
Informativ Payments plugs into every touchpoint — from lead forms and AI chat to ad platforms and online desking. Partners can show accurate, credit-qualified payments across the entire shopper journey.

VALUE PROP 7 — Speed, Freshness, and Reliability:
Engine updates incentives, taxes, lender programs, and dealer rules in near real-time and responds in milliseconds. No stale data. No caching delays. No performance drag — just clean, live, fundable numbers.

THE OUTCOME:
A modern, API-driven payment experience that builds trust with shoppers, raises dealer profitability, improves conversion, reduces surprises and re-desks, and differentiates the partner platform instantly. Informativ Payments turns affordability into a competitive advantage.

chunks.title: Verified STIPS Prospecting Talk Tracks — Compliance Customer (Full Playbook)
chunks.content:
VERIFIED STIPS — Compliance Customer Prospecting Playbook

OPENING (positive acknowledgement + tease upgrade):
"I see you're already using Informativ Compliance, so your stip collection process is secure — that's great. We just launched an upgrade called Verified STIPS powered by TurboPass that takes that process and removes the last bits of back-and-forth with the customer. Can I share how it's speeding up funding for other dealers?"

POSITION AS NEXT-LEVEL ENHANCEMENT:
"Today, you're already collecting stips in a compliant way. Verified STIPS simply automates the verification part — pulling income, residence, and employment data directly from the source in real-time, so your team isn't waiting on uploads or chasing customers for missing docs."

FUNDING SPEED ANGLE:
"Your current system is secure — Verified STIPS just makes it faster. Dealers using it are cutting stip collection time from days to minutes, which means contracts are funded faster and deals are off your books sooner."

PHONE SCRIPT (ROI-driven):
Step 1 — Warm Acknowledgement: "Hi [First Name], it's [Your Name] with Informativ. You're already using Informativ Compliance for secure stip collection — which puts you ahead of the curve. We've now added an upgrade called Verified STIPS powered by TurboPass that takes that process and makes it instant."
Step 2 — ROI Hook: "Lenders get the data directly from the source — speeds up decisioning and funding, and in some cases triggers rebates on origination fees because the stip is pre-verified."
Step 3 — Question: "If you could cut stip collection time from days to minutes — and potentially qualify for lender fee rebates — how much impact would that have on your month-end close?"
Step 4 — Close: "Since you're already on Informativ Compliance, adding Verified STIPS is simple — no process change. Let's grab 15 minutes with your Regional Sales Manager. Does [Day/Time] work?"

EMAIL SUBJECT LINES:
- "Faster funding + lender fee rebates"
- "Your stip process — now a profit center"
- "Upgrade to instant, verified stips"

EMAIL BODY (short upgrade pitch):
"Hi [First Name], you're already collecting stips securely with Informativ Compliance — Verified STIPS powered by TurboPass is the upgrade that can turn that process into a real ROI driver. Dealers upgrading are seeing: stip collection time cut from days to minutes; faster lender decisioning and contract funding; rebates on origination fees from some lenders due to pre-verified docs; elimination of document fraud from falsified stips; a modern, no-upload experience customers love. Since you're already on Informativ Compliance, setup is simple. Can we schedule a quick 15-minute walk-through?"

VOICEMAIL (20 sec): "Hi [First Name], it's [Your Name] with Informativ. You're already using Informativ Compliance for secure stip collection. We've added Verified STIPS — it verifies income, residence, and employment instantly, speeds up funding, and in some cases earns lenders' origination fee rebates. I'll send you an email, but let's set up 15 minutes."

chunks.title: Verified STIPS Prospecting Talk Tracks — Credit-Only Customer (Full Playbook)
chunks.content:
VERIFIED STIPS — Credit-Only Customer Prospecting Playbook

OPENING (recognition + problem statement):
"I see you're using Informativ Credit for your bureau pulls — so you already trust us with your credit data. I want to share a new add-on, Verified STIPS powered by TurboPass, that lets you collect and verify income, residence, and employment instantly, without chasing customers for paperwork. And the best part — you can send them straight into RouteOne or DealerTrack, so your current process doesn't change."

TIE TO CURRENT PAIN POINTS:
"Right now, most dealers are still asking customers to email, text, or upload stips. That delays decisioning, slows funding, and leaves a window for fake docs to slip through. Verified STIPS closes that gap — data comes directly from the issuing source."

ROI HOOK:
"Dealers adding Verified STIPS are seeing: funding times cut from days to minutes; reduced risk of chargebacks and fraud losses; happier customers who don't have to upload a thing; in some cases, lender origination fee rebates for pre-verified docs."

PHONE SCRIPT:
Step 1 — Warm Intro: "Hi [First Name], this is [Your Name] with Informativ. I see you're using us for credit — I'm calling because we have a new tool, Verified STIPS powered by TurboPass, that plugs right into RouteOne or DealerTrack to verify customer stips instantly."
Step 2 — Value & ROI: "It's a secure, automated way to confirm income, residence, and employment directly from the source — no uploading or emailing from the customer, no fake docs, funding in minutes instead of days. Some lenders even rebate origination fees when they get pre-verified stips."
Step 3 — Close: "It's quick to set up and doesn't require changing your process. Can we grab 15 minutes so I can show you how it works with RouteOne or DealerTrack?"

EMAIL SUBJECT: "Instant Verified STIPS — now in RouteOne & DealerTrack"

EMAIL BODY:
"Hi [First Name], you're already using Informativ Credit — now you can add Verified STIPS powered by TurboPass to: verify stips instantly from the issuing source; eliminate fake docs and reduce fraud risk; cut funding times from days to minutes; deliver a modern, no-upload customer experience; in some cases, earn lender origination fee rebates. Verified STIPS works right inside RouteOne or DealerTrack — no change to your workflow. Can we set up a quick 15-minute walk-through?"

KEY ROI POINTS FOR SDRs:
- Prevent Fraud — verified data from source, no customer-provided docs to manipulate
- Speed Decisioning & Funding — minutes, not days
- Modernize CX — no scanning, emailing, or uploads for the customer
- Revenue Opportunity — potential lender origination fee rebates
- No Workflow Change — fits into RouteOne/DealerTrack exactly like current stips

chunks.title: Informativ Flips Compliance From an Expense to a Profit
chunks.content:
INFORMATIV FLIPS COMPLIANCE FROM AN EXPENSE TO A PROFIT

The Industry's FIRST & ONLY platform that flips compliance from an expense to a profit.

Informativ's platform is proven to help dealers SELL MORE CARS, MAKE MORE MONEY, SAVE MONEY, and lock down a consistent compliant process so you keep more of what you make.

WE DELIVER IT ALL FOR ONE FIXED FLAT MONTHLY RATE.

WE DO IT BY INTEGRATING:
- Our FLAT-RATE CREDIT REPORTING SOLUTION that includes all credit pulls & credit-related compliance
- Our PROACTIVE COMPLIANCE TECH that protects PII, controls the sales process & fulfills FTC regulations
- Our ID & SYNTHETIC FRAUD DETECTION & PREVENTION TECHNOLOGY that protects dealerships from theft
- Our innovative QR CODE LEAD ENRICHMENT SOLUTION that generates verified & prequalified leads

KEY POSITIONING LANGUAGE:
"We flip compliance from an expense to a profit" — use this as the opening anchor in executive-level conversations where the dealer views compliance as a cost center. The flat-rate model is the mechanism: by including all credit pulls and credit-related compliance under one fixed monthly rate, Informativ converts variable, unpredictable compliance + bureau spend into a predictable line item AND surfaces savings opportunities (no per-pull costs, no per-document compliance fees).

WHEN TO USE:
- CFO conversations (predictability + savings)
- Multi-rooftop discussions (consistent flat rate across all locations)
- Dealers fatigued by bureau price increases (flat-rate insulates them)

Prospecting
Objection: New product/upgrade to TS will bring changes to internal processes.
Rebuttal:
Totally fair — but the goal isn’t to add more process, it’s to remove the most time-wasting, repetitive steps. Informativ automates compliance, reduces rework, and makes the process consistent across reps. We replace manual friction with guided, faster workflows.
Objection: Compliance doesn’t matter now that the CARS rule is dead.
Rebuttal:
CARS rule aside, the FTC Safeguards Rule is very much alive — and it’s enforceable. Beyond regulation, compliance is how you protect your business from lawsuits, chargebacks, and public exposure.
Objection: We’ve never experienced fraud.
Rebuttal:
That’s great — but fraud is growing fast. Synthetic IDs, mobile-first scams, and social engineering are harder to detect. Informativ puts controls in place before you become a target.
Objection: Not adding any new expenses. (Prospecting/Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
What if we could lower your overall cost by replacing 2–3 separate vendors with one? Informativ isn’t just a new expense — it’s a smarter investment with real ROI.
Objection: Too many logins and systems already. (Prospecting/Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
Informativ was designed to reduce system creep. One login, one guided workflow — no toggling between platforms or chasing down paperwork.
Discovery
Objection: I want the flexibility to run as many credit pulls as I want.
Rebuttal:
That flexibility comes at a cost — literally. Most dealers overspend on unnecessary pulls that don’t fund. We help you qualify customers smarter, reduce cost per deal, and still give you override options when needed.
Objection: I don’t want to change my process.
Rebuttal:
You don’t have to overhaul everything — but if the current process is costing you time, money, or risk exposure, isn’t it worth seeing if there’s a better way? We’ve helped teams streamline what they already do without disrupting their flow.
Objection: Not all stores operate the same, need setup flexibility.
Rebuttal:
No problem — our platform scales to each store’s structure. You can standardize where it helps (compliance, fraud prevention) while customizing access, workflows, and reporting by location.
Objection: Your system doesn’t fit my process.
Rebuttal:
That’s exactly what we solve for. We adapt to your current workflow and eliminate steps that

That’s common — most tools were built for IT, not the sales desk. Informativ is intuitive, fast, and designed for real-world use. We also support your launch with training and reinforcement to drive adoption.

Perfect. Then Informativ becomes the upgrade — with compliance triggers, ID checks, and real-time fraud alerts layered into your deal jacket for added protection.

Many claim that — few deliver the full solution. Informativ is credit, fraud, and compliance in one unified platform — with real-time protections and full audit trails. Let’s compare side-by-side.

What if we could lower your overall cost by replacing 2–3 separate vendors with one? Informativ isn’t just a new expense — it’s a smarter investment with real ROI.
Objection: Too many logins and systems already. (Prospecting/Discovery/TD)

Informativ was designed to reduce system creep. One login, one guided workflow — no toggling between platforms or chasing down paperwork.
TD
Objection: I need a different hard pull for my credit unions.

We get that — and our platform supports the ability to segment pulls based on lender requirements. Informativ is designed to work with your funding partners, not against them.
Objection: I’ve always done 3 hard pulls — that’s what my lenders accept. (TD/Pricing)

Totally makes sense, and we can support that workflow. But let’s talk about how many of those are actually needed to close the deal. Soft pull + intelligent hard pull strategy can cut cost and get lenders what they need — without unnecessary spend.

Informativ is built for flexibility. We support multi-user, multi-location access and permissions — ideal for roaming reps or sales floors that shift teams.

Our digital jacket supports full FTC compliance and key deal docs. Where OEM or DMS limitations exist, we integrate around them — so your team can still stay digital and compliant.
Objection: I need more than one hard pull within 30 days.

Objection: This adds another step in the process. (Discovery/TD)

Objection: I can’t get my team to use the tools we already have. (Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
That’s common — most tools were built for IT, not the sales desk. Informativ is intuitive, fast, and designed for real-world use. We also support your launch with training and reinforcement to drive adoption.
Objection: Price and disrupting our current process.

We reduce cost and simplify process — not the other way around. Most dealers find we cut monthly tech costs and reduce effort at the same time.
Objection: We were told all documents would be digitized.

That’s the goal — and we deliver. Some documents may still need to originate from external systems, but Informativ brings them together in one secure, audit-ready digital jacket.
Objection: I need more than one hard pull for CUs.
Rebuttal:
And we support that. Informativ lets you configure how, when, and where pulls happen — so your credit union relationships stay intact without overspending on every deal.
Objection: We have a provider that offers the same services. (Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
Many claim that — few deliver the full solution. Informativ is credit, fraud, and compliance in one unified platform — with real-time protections and full audit trails. Let’s compare side-by-side.
Objection: We were sold on seamless integration — but we’ve seen issues.

That’s why we build native integrations — not surface-level ones. We’ll work with your current system to fix duplication, sync data correctly, and reduce friction.
Objection: Too many logins and systems already. (Prospecting/Discovery/TD)
Rebuttal:
Informativ was designed to reduce system creep. One login, one guided workflow — no toggling between platforms or chasing down paperwork.
Pricing
Objection: Too expensive.
Rebuttal:
Most of our dealers recover their investment in the first few months — whether by preventing one fraud case, reducing bureau spend, or improving lead-to-sale conversion. It’s not an expense — it’s a smarter way to operate.
Objection: You can’t compete with 700 Credit on price.
Rebuttal:
Price matters — but value matters more. Informativ offers not just credit but compliance, fraud protection, and sales enablement in one platform. 700 Credit is one slice. We’re the full solution.
Objection: I feel like I’m paying more and getting less. (TD/Pricing)
Rebuttal:
Let’s break it down. Are you paying for multiple disconnected tools that don’t talk to each other? We bundle it into one streamlined platform with more visibility, fewer steps, and stronger ROI.
Objection: Owner is a penny pincher.

Then they’ll love Informativ. Most of our clients actually cut spend by consolidating credit, fraud, and compliance tools into one platform. Let’s walk through what they’re paying today vs. our flat-rate model.
Objection: I’m a small dealer — this is more than I’m spending now.
Rebuttal:
Understandable. But what if we could protect you from one fraud case, or automate a compliance issue that could cost thousands? Informativ was built to scale — small stores benefit the most from smart automation and protection.
Objection: Just too expensive.

When you factor in bureau fees, fraud risk, compliance gaps, and wasted time — Informativ usually pays for itself in the first 60 days. We also offer tiered options that meet dealers where they are.

I’ve already trained my staff.
The landscape is changing….
What could possibly go wrong between the classroom and the showroom?
Hopefully we’ve all made the investment in training and reviewing the behavior of our staff with the standards we specify in our written policies and procedures.  These should go hand in hand and be done on a consistent basis.  Speaking from experience, the gaps between what we expect and what actually happens in our showrooms still varies greatly from dealership to dealership.  Let’s consider some facts.
Compliance is not optional
Training is temporary.
Turnover is consistent.
Speed and transparency are a necessity today to stay competitive.
Fraud is on the uptick. (NJCAR conducted a recent event on this very topic)
The new landscape includes “virtual” remote deals and “Digital Retailing”.
Sources now include walk in, web applications, and Digital retailing, typically starting the transaction in 3 different platforms, YET the necessary steps for compliance are consistent regardless of where the engagement starts! We don’t get to skip steps because things change.
As a result, behavior is often inconsistent, incomplete, and erratic.
Let’s go through a typical car sales process, and identify some of the risks, as well as some real-life results of non-compliance.
The meet and greet
We engage with the consumer and at some point ask for a Driver’s License and maybe an Insurance Card.  Upon collection we immediately have the responsibility to Safeguard that information as per the promises in our Privacy Statement and the requirements of the GLB Safeguards Rule.  We must also retain this information for the subscribed period of time depending on the outcome of the engagement.  What are my risks in this simple process?
Could be a fraudulent license
Could be an expired license
The copy could be lost, misplaced, stolen, thrown away (all of which violate your Privacy Statement and Federal Safeguards requirements).
How is this info requested and received “remotely”?  Are images stored in a salesperson cell phone or personal e-mail account?
Possible outcomes – (1) A dealership in Pennsylvania settled a class action lawsuit where 150 victims had their identities stolen for $2.45 Million.  (2) NJ Theft Ring stole more than 30 cars Online in July of 2020.  How many are getting through without being caught across the country?
The Credit Application
When our sales team starts the credit application (and hopefully the Privacy Statement), what are the risks?
If you are still completing a paper credit application, you are creating a document that is a $42,380 liability if left unprotected under the Safeguards Rule.  The same liability that was created during the test drive when collecting the Driver’s license and insurance card.
Even if you are printing the app out of your new car/used car pack in your CRM platform, you are still creating that paper liability.
Handwritten credit apps can be inconsistent, incomplete, or illegible, all creating problems when you get ready to pull the consumers credit. And you never know who’s handwriting is on that app or worse yet, changes to the app.
There have been instances where the information on the Credit App “changed” or was modified. In some cases, this is legitimate, while others have unfortunately led to fraud.  September 8, 2020 FTC judgement against Tate Auto Group for $7.3M and forced to terminate 4 dealerships for exactly this reason.  How long does it take you to generate $7M in NET profit, and how much capital is tied up in 4 dealerships?
Time to check credit
To pull a consumer’s credit, we have regulatory responsibility of the Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) Red Flags Rule to verify who they are and to obtain their authorization prior to pulling their credit (Permissible Purpose).  What could possibly go wrong with this?
Is your desk verifying each customer’ paperwork, ID, and consent prior to running credit to support FCRA requirements?
Is your desk giving the same level of administrative attention to the deals that DON’T cross the finish line (Dead Deals), in the collection, protection, and retention to support the requirements under the Fair and Accurate Credit Transaction Act (FACTA)?
Is your desk clearing/resolving ALL open Red Flags, OFAC requirements, and Notice requirements (Privacy Notice, Credit Score Disclosure Notice and Adverse Action Notice)?
Are you checking for synthetic fraud when pulling credit?
Possible liability – Three examples come to mind for non-adherence. The first that went public dealt with 16 boxes of dead deal jackets being stolen from a dealership.  Another similar case involved someone approaching an employee at a dealership and paying $50 each for 100 credit apps. The third example included a dealership that settled for $270,000 with an attorney over the inability to prove they had consent on “ONE” dead deal.  Is your dealership capable of putting their hands on every consumer you ran credit on over the last 5 years?  Your Privacy Statement guarantees your customer that level of protection.
The ongoing Battle of Contracts in-Transit and Collecting Information
In many cases the deal needs information from the customer to get the deal funded after the vehicle has been delivered.  In deals where STIPS are required by the lender, how do we collect and protect this sensitive information in a platform that is secure and immediate?
I frequently see STIPS and/or ID’s, insurance cards, being texted to a salespersons phone, or emailed to their private e-mail account.  STOP!!!!!  This is a major violation of your Privacy and Safeguards Policy (yes, it is still a violation even though they are still working for you).
Where are we headed with all of this?
Take all the above and add into the mix our web leads, whatever your Digital Retailing game plan is, and multiply that by the number of locations you own, and it becomes a daunting challenge.  The collateral damage to your brand could easily trump the temporary financial pain of a fine or penalty.
The time for technology to control and administer this mind-numbing maze is long overdue.  Without automating and enforcing the entire process these liabilities will continue.  Platforms currently exist that empower real-time actionable intelligence to enforce, monitor, eliminate rogue behavior, and maintain a compliant environment.  Every other department in the dealership is already automated (in many cases with dozens of vendors), yet the risks to our brand and our profitability in our variable operations are still largely manual. If you’re in the group that’s been struggling with some of these behaviors and haven’t already searched for a platform to put your showroom on autopilot, now is the time. There are tools to address all the above in a very affordable manner.  There is an easier way!
Dealer Safeguards Solutions (DSGSS)
Dealer Safeguards Solutions has given dealerships across the country the ability to leverage an innovative, comprehensive control and protection platform for compliance and fraud.  Their strategic partnerships deliver the most comprehensive digital compliance enforcement platform that locks down your people, paperwork, processes, and policies to protect your dealership, brand, and customers.
Dealers have found the DSGSS software solution has helped to take their sales process and turn it on autopilot when addressing all issues outlined in this article.  The program has proven to be the go-to COVID Remote solution to securely transact. It has been the key to maintaining a compliant environment during this very sensitive time.
For more information or to schedule a short demo, please reach out to Tom Meredith -  Regional Director.  tom@dsgss.com  617-833-9121.

Unacceptable Business Types:
Verify that the company is NOT a business listed below:
1. Bail Bond Enforcement or Bounty Hunters
2. Internet people locator services
3. Diet centers
4. Adoption search firms
5. Credit Repair companies or credit clinics
6. Credit Counseling
7. Loan Modification Companies
8. Debt Settlement Companies
9. Investigative companies, including private investigators and detective agencies
10. Media agencies, news agencies or journalists
11. Law enforcement
12. Non-governmental agencies or businesses associated with the collection of child
support
13. Businesses operating out of a residence
14. Dating Services
15. Asset Location Services
16. Attorney or Law Firm in the practice of Law or any other legal services of any kind
17. Condominium/Homeowners Associations
18. Future services (i.e., health clubs, continuity clubs, etc.)
19. Country Clubs
20. Timeshare
21. Companies involved and/or associated with inappropriate adult content web sites and/
or adult-type telephone services
22. Any company or individual who is known to have been involved in credit fraud or other
unethical business practices
23. Adult entertainment service of any kind
24. Child location service (i.e. company that locates missing children)
25. Financial Counseling / Consumer Dept Counseling
26. Foreign company or agency of a foreign government
27. Genealogical or heir research firm
28. Massage service
29. Pawn Shop
30. Repossession company
31. Subscriptions (magazines, book clubs, record clubs, etc.)
32. Tattoo service
33. Weapons dealer, seller or distributor
34. Brokering, Reselling, Releasing Credit Reports
35. Diet centers
36. Internet People Locator Services
37. Process Server
38. TU added "Debt Consolidation" as of 10/7/2020/lc (per conversation with TU)
Per George Cooper (10/13/20) All of the companies listed below are the
same. If they state anything on their website about their core business

chunks.content:
IS YOUR SALES DEPARTMENT
COMPLIANT?
Under the FTC Safeguards Rule, your sales department
could be exposing you to fines of over $100,000 per violation.
The compliance requirements can have a huge impact on your
dealership if you’re not prepared and WILL result in devastating fines.
Issues you should be concerned about:
Is your . . .
Sales staff storing consumer information on their CELL PHONE or personal email?
Really, it’s not just sales staff. At a recent NADA Show, a GM confessed to having hundreds of driver’s
license photos on his phone. This seemingly “easy” way to capture info is extremely costly. By using a
solution with an app (like ours), you can quickly capture the driver’s license info you need to
complete the deal.
Do employees . . .
Leave private consumer information EXPOSED in un-secure locations?
Think - salesperson’s desk, the copy machine, private email accounts. All of this exposed personal
information leaves you at risk for violations and hefty fines. Our software leverages Secure Link
Technology: A platform that almost completely eliminates paper and protects your dealership and
your customers from potential exposure to data breaches.
Can you . . .
Instantly identify FRAUDULENT drivers' licenses and other fake documents?
Unfortunately, fraud has become more prevalent in recent months and criminals are driving away with
dealership vehicles at an alarming rate. Our software solution instantly provides protection the minute
a driver's license is uploaded.
Are you . . .
Able to reliably store “dead deals” for the REQUIRED 5 YEARS?
That’s a lot of deals with a lot of personal information that you’re responsible for storing, protecting, and
PROVING that you’re retaining. Our solution stores the dead deal jackets electronically and supports
your compliance checks.

“Today, you’re already collecting stips in a compliant way. Verified Stips simply automates the verification part — pulling income, residence, and employment data directly from the source in real-time, so your team isn’t waiting on uploads or chasing customers for missing docs.”

chunks.source: Fakes, Fraud & Faster Funding 5 Shocking Ways Document Fraud Is Costing You
chunks.filename: Fakes, Fraud & Faster Funding 5 Shocking Ways Document Fraud Is Costing You.pdf
chunks.content:
FAKES, FRAUD & FASTER FUNDING:
5 SHOCKING WAYS DOCUMENT
FRAUD IS COSTING YOU
In 2024, Visa conducted a study of fraud in the U.S. that found document fraud exploded 244%—
and your dealership is in the crosshairs. From fake pay stubs to phony insurance cards to forged
utility bills, fraud is harder to detect and easier to commit. Every bad deal costs time, money, and
credibility with lenders.
But you’re not powerless. Informativ’s Verified Stips, powered by TurboPass, lets you collect
and validate the most critical documents in seconds—directly from the source. Here are five
hard-hitting reasons to ditch the paper chase and protect your profit.
STOP FRAUD BEFORE IT STARTS
1
What’s Happening: Fraudsters are using online templates, Photoshop, and AI-generated
documents to fake their way into car deals.
Why It Matters: Your F&I team is under pressure—and fraudsters know it. A single bad deal can
lead to chargebacks, bank fees, or even a repo.
How Informativ Helps: With Verified Stips, documents are pulled instantly from payroll providers,
banks, insurers, and utilities—verified at the source, not by sight. It’s part of our multi-layered fraud
prevention platform that protects every step of your process, from the first lead to final funding.
No screenshots. No fakes. Just real data from real sources—verified and secure.
GET FUNDED FASTER
2
What’s Happening: Manual document collection slows down your deals. Every back-and-forth
adds friction and gives customers time to walk away.
Why It Matters: Speed is everything in the showroom. A smoother path to funding equals more
closed deals. By integrating with existing platforms like Informativ’s Compliance solution,
RouteOne, and Dealertrack, Verified Stips fits seamlessly into your existing workflow—so you don’t
have to change your process, just speed it up.
How Informativ Helps: Get verified proof of income, employment, insurance, and residence in
seconds. Verified Stips helps you structure deals faster—and get funded without the friction.
CUT COMPLIANCE RISK BY 50–80%
3

chunks.title: Why Soft Pulls Alone Are Not Enough — Compliance & Legal Risk
chunks.content:
WHY SOFT PULLS ALONE ARE NOT ENOUGH — Compliance & Legal Positioning

LEGAL DISCLAIMER: Informational only — not legal advice. Auto financing laws vary by jurisdiction.

THE PROBLEM:
While soft pulls are useful for prequalification and consumer engagement, relying SOLELY on soft pulls instead of hard inquiries presents several risks for dealerships, lenders, and consumers.

RISK 1 — INCOMPLETE CREDIT DECISIONING:
- Soft pulls do not show full credit details (closed accounts, some trade lines, collections)
- Lenders require hard pulls to make a final lending decision and assess risk accurately
- Lenders will almost always run their own hard pull anyway — what happens if something shows on the hard pull that wasn't on the soft pull? Terms change after the fact, causing dealership issues and increased CIT
- Consumers may receive misleading financing offers based on incomplete data

RISK 2 — COMPLIANCE RISKS (FCRA & ECOA VIOLATIONS):
- If a credit decision is made (denial or higher loan terms) based on a soft pull, the dealer is LEGALLY REQUIRED to:
  - Send an Adverse Action Notice (if financing is denied or offered on significantly worse terms)
  - Provide a Risk-Based Pricing Notice (if the consumer gets a higher interest rate due to credit score risk)
- Failure to issue these notices can result in regulatory scrutiny, lawsuits, and FCRA/ECOA violations
- Red Flags and Out-of-Wallet questions are currently only supported on Hard Pulls — without hard pulls, dealers must build custom automation to avoid risk

RISK 3 — POTENTIAL CONSUMER HARM:
- Consumers may not realize their credit report impacted their loan terms if AA/RBPN notices are not issued
- Without a hard pull, consumers lose the ability to dispute inaccurate credit data that affects their loan terms
- Rate shopping becomes more difficult — auto credit scoring models are designed to accommodate multiple hard pulls within a short window

RISK 4 — IMPACT ON CREDIT SCORE MODELS:
- Auto lenders and bureaus rely on hard pulls to assess borrower risk accurately
- Risk-based pricing models factor in recent auto inquiries to determine whether a consumer is shopping responsibly or exhibiting financial distress
- If dealerships stop performing hard pulls, it could distort risk models — impacting approval rates, interest rates, and credit reporting accuracy

RISK 5 — DEALERSHIP & LENDER RISKS:
- If a lender later performs a hard pull and finds discrepancies, the financing deal could be delayed or denied
- Regulatory bodies (FTC, CFPB) may investigate dealerships that rely solely on soft pulls for credit decisioning
- Dealers face increased fraud risks — soft pulls do not always detect synthetic identities or hidden delinquencies

POSITIONING CONCLUSION:
- Soft pulls = great for prequalification and consumer engagement
- Soft pulls = INSUFFICIENT for final lending decisions, compliance, and risk-based pricing
- Eliminating hard pulls would disrupt credit risk modeling, cause compliance issues, and reduce credit transparency

USE THIS WHEN:
- A competitor positions "soft-pull-only" as a feature — frame it as a compliance risk
- A dealer asks "can we just stop running hard pulls to save money?" — explain the FCRA/ECOA implications
- Defending why Control (full hard + soft) is the safest enterprise tier
- Helping Elevate prospects understand why transactional hard pulls (not zero hard pulls) is the right model

chunks.content:
informativ.com
CASE STUDY
Cavender Auto Family prevents fraud, enforces compliance
and cuts costs with Informativ’s Total Solution.
Cavender Auto Family has 17 dealerships across Texas and Oklahoma. The third-generation family-run
dealership has enjoyed 80+ years of success by focusing on the people they help not the products they sell.
CHALLENGE
Ensuring consistency and controlling costs was difficult across 17 dealerships. Additionally, with the
increasing threat of fraud and added compliance requirements of the FTC Safeguards Rule, the Cavender
team knew they needed a technology partner to help them achieve their goals, protect their customers’
information, and their dealerships.
SOLUTION
Cavender Auto Family partnered with Informativ for the Total Solution to enforce compliance, prevent
fraud, and control costs.
Compliance Made Easy
Informativ’s compliance technology removes paperwork from the showroom and collects, creates, and stores all
customer information digitally. The technology creates guardrails for the sales team which proactively enforces
compliant deals. It also controls access to customer information and the ability to change any data.
“The full suite of solutions that Informativ provides is great. We were disjointed before and now that it's all
under one umbrella it’s easier for the stores to manage, easier for us from an audit perspective to dial in what's
happening. Having everything from the mobile scanning of the driver's license and doing all the documents
digitally — signing them and pushing them over to the deal jacket — is awesome.”
Ben Stillwagon, Director of IT
Cavender Auto Family
The ONLY Complete & Proactive Credit,
Compliance & Customer Insights Solution 800.448.0183 | info@informativ.com

informativ.com
Fraud Prevention That Works
Informativ’s fraud prevention technology includes multiple verifications and validations throughout the sales process,
but always begins before the sales team spends time working a deal. Whether in-person or remote, Informativ’s fraud
technology scans a customer’s driver’s license verifying the data within the ID’s barcode as well as the information on the
license.
“The driver’s license validation piece has really saved us a few times,” said Ben Stillwagon, Director of IT at Cavender.
“One example is a couple at one of our stores in West Texas was trying to steal two G-wagons. Those are very expensive,
$200,000 cars. The driver’s license came back fraudulent! We kept them there as we worked through everything with
law enforcement, and they showed up and took them away in handcuffs. So, it was really a success story – and we’ve
seen that several times!”
Soft Pulls Save Time
The Total Solution enabled Cavender Auto Family to save time by understanding if the buyer is good upfront through a
soft pull prequalification. Informativ’s soft pulls include the same information as a hard pull but don’t harm the
customer’s credit and don’t have the same compliance obligations as a hard pull. Cavender made minor changes to
their deal sheet so the soft pulls would come first to understand the customer’s buying ability. Then the deal goes back
to the desk.
“So doing soft pulls was a game changer for us,” said Stillwagon. “That was probably the biggest win for us because
we were doing hard pulls on everybody, whether they were going to buy a car from us or not.”
Saving Money Is a Game Changer
Informativ’s Total Solution includes credit, compliance, and fraud prevention for one flat monthly rate – often less than or
equal to what a dealer is paying just for credit alone.
“We were new to it being all under one umbrella and the cost savings that comes with that -- it's been amazing,” said
Stillwagon. “We're seeing an incredible amount of savings and in this business right now, anywhere we can find extra
pennies to help our bottom line has been really beneficial. It’s a game changer for us.”
Ensuring Success
Informativ is a true partner. Our team worked with the Cavender team to understand their business and how we could
complement – not disrupt – their process.
“Onboarding and training were actually really awesome,” said Stillwagon. “We had some requirements that probably
weren’t typical of an install and the Informativ team was able to pivot to our sales process. The whole team just really
held our salespeople’s hands through the entire process.”

chunks.content:
Rules of Engagement for Informativ-CUDL Partnership
Lead Definition: A lead is defined as a prospect who has been contacted and has expressed interest in learning more about the services offered by Informativ.
Lead Attribution:
Submission Process: The referring party must document leads via a webform submission.  Each company will maintain its own webform to document referrals in its system of record. The CUDL Referral Form can be found here.
Evaluation of Client Status: Upon receiving the lead, Revenue Operations will validate if they are a current Client.
If the rooftop is a current Client, the lead will be disqualified if there is no cross-sell potential. Clients cannot be on these packages:
Total Solution
Control
Protect
If they are a Prospect OR Client is not using the above flat rate packages, then RevOps will evaluate existing opportunities.
Evaluation of Existing Opportunities: Upon receiving a lead, Revenue Operations will evaluate if there is an existing opportunity in progress for that lead:
If an opportunity exists and there is verified activity (phone conversations or meetings) within the last 30 days, the opportunity will remain with the company managing it.
Automated mass communications or marketing emails do not qualify as verified activity.
No Existing Opportunity: If no existing opportunity with verified activity exists, the lead will be attributed to the referring party in the system of record.
Daily reports will be sent to CUDL. These include:
Inbound Leads & Corresponding Status
Opportunities w/ Status
Closed Won Opportunities
Active Clients
Each company will provide the other party with monthly reports on documented referrals and referrals received to ensure alignment.
Referral Follow-Up Policy
Post-Assignment Rules: Once an account is reassigned to the referring party, it is off-limits for the internal sales team to pursue.
Lead must be engaged within 24 hours.
In cases of disputes, Management will make the final determination.
If Management identifies that the Sales Rep is failing to engage with Leads within the agreed 24 hour turn-around-time, the Lead may be subject to reassignment.
Initial Acknowledgment
Email or text sent within 4 hours of receiving the referral.
Referrals received after 5pm local time must be acknowledged by 9am next business day.
First Contact Attempt
Phone call within 12 hours of referral receipt.
If after hours, call must occur by 12pm next business day.
Second Contact Attempt
If no response, a second call and email must be completed within 24 hours of lead receipt.
Required Outcome Within 48 Hours
Reps must achieve one of the following within 48 hours of receiving the referral:
Solution Fit Scheduled or Completed
Tech Demo Scheduled or Completed
If no outcome is met within 48 hours, the referral enters escalation.
Documentation Requirements
All activities must be documented in Salesforce (calls, emails, demos, notes).
Salesforce documentation is the official source of truth for compliance.
If it is not documented in Salesforce, it is considered not done.
Escalation & Reassignment Rules
Escalations
At 24 hours: Referral flagged for risk.
At 48 hours: Sales Manager notified; rep must provide explanation and action plan.  That plan must be delivered and agreed to by the VP of Business Development.
Reassignment
Referrals may be reassigned if either occurs:
No documented attempts in Salesforce within 24 hours, OR
Required outcome (solution fit or scheduled demo) is not achieved and documented within 48 hours.
Communication with the VP of Business Development about a non-responsive referral.
Repeated non-compliance (2 or more per month) triggers removal from referral rotation.
Reassignment After “Closed Lost”: If an opportunity is marked as “Closed Lost” or deemed non-viable, the prospect becomes eligible to be pursued again by either the Referring Party or the assigned Regional Sales Manager (RSM).
Commission Splits: The Company reserves the right to establish a commission split with its internal sales team to help close a deal.  Management will determine this.
Attribution Rules
Attribution between Informativ and CUDL depends on who generated the lead and completed the sale.
If within (6) Months of the Referral Date, a referral enters into an agreement with Informativ, the CUDL team will be compensated.
Compensation Plan for Informativ Sales Team
All CUDL opportunities will count towards a separate quota (at an organization-wide level) in addition to the assigned quota for 2025.
Sales team members will be compensated at the agreed upon rates outlined in their Compensation Plan.
Commission Splits – All opportunities are subject to a standard 50/50 split between the Regional Sales Manager and the Affiliate Team.
Quota: 50/50
Commissions: 50/50
Bundled Bonus: 50/50
Escalation and Dispute Resolution
All disputes regarding lead attribution, opportunity assignment, or commission splits will be escalated to Management for final determination.
Both parties must maintain transparency and professional communication during the dispute-resolution process.
Collaboration Guidelines
Informativ sales team members must coordinate with CUDL counterparts to ensure a seamless customer experience.
Regular check-ins and updates between the two teams should be facilitated to avoid overlaps or conflicts in lead handling.
Client Management
Independent of who closes the opportunity, once the Client is installed, the account will be owned by the affiliate department until termination unless otherwise specified by management.
Summary of Key Points
Leads must be submitted through a web form and evaluated for existing opportunities.
Attribution depends on verified activity within the last 30 days.
Once a lead is assigned, it is off-limits to the other party unless the opportunity becomes “Closed Lost.”
Licensing fees and commissions are determined by who generates the lead and completes the sale.
Leads must close within 6 months of the referral date to qualify for payment.
Commission/Quota Splits are a 50/50 split between the Sales/Affiliate Team members.

the customer experience.

Dealer orders a compliant soft pull credit report from Informativ within CarNow
Customer completes required disclosures and consent digitally
Credit insight is returned without disrupting the conversation
Customer journey continues seamlessly to showroom or next steps

Understand credit readiness sooner and tailor responses and next
steps based on real data
Faster qualification during live conversations
More productive digital engagements
Better-prepared customers entering the deal with fewer surprises
later in the process
Reduced regulatory and audit risk
Embedded soft pull credit ordering without redirects or platform
switching
BOTTOM LINE:
Informativ’s integration with CarNow empowers dealers to access compliant credit
insight directly within digital conversations—helping them engage smarter, qualify
earlier, and move customers seamlessly from online to showroom.
800.448.0183 sales@informativ.com informativ.com

chunks.source: Informativ's API Integration with VinSolutions Prevents Duplicates & Streamlines Lead Creation from Driver’s License Scans

chunks.content:
API Integration
Prevent Duplicates & Streamline Lead Creation from Driver’s License Scans
Informativ’s integration with the VinSolutions API combines driver’s license scanning, real-
time fraud screening, and instant matching through VinSolutions’ API to prevent
duplicate leads in your CRM. Sales teams capture leads confidently—selecting existing
records when matches are found or creating new, clean records when they’re not—all
from a single mobile workflow.
Key Benefits
Duplicate Prevention
Real-time matching against VIN Solutions’
database avoids redundant records and
maintains CRM integrity.
Faster Lead Qualification
Instant fraud screening and record lookup
ensure only high-quality, unique leads enter
your pipeline.
Seamless Mobile Experience
Salespeople scan a driver’s license, review
matches, and finalize lead actions entirely on
their device—no desktop required.
Improved Data Accuracy
Standardized driver’s license parsing plus
VinSolutions’ enrichment reduces manual
errors and fills in missing details.
Consistent CRM Hygiene
By eliminating duplicate entries, reporting
and analytics remain reliable and actionable.
800.448.0183 hello@informativ.com informativ.com

chunks.source: Informativ's Integration with Tekion CRM & DMS
chunks.filename: Informativ's Integration with Tekion CRM & DMS.pdf
chunks.content:
Integration
CRM Integration
Informativ’s integration with Tekion CRM ensures your dealership’s customer data flows
seamlessly between the platforms. This reduces manual data entry and duplicates.
All leads from all sources are supported by the integration.
The integration polls Tekion every 5 minutes for new and/or updated leads.
When a driver’s license is scanned using Informativ’s mobile app, matching
suggestions are presented to avoid duplicates.
DMS Integration
Informativ’s direct connection to Tekion’s DMS effortlessly pushes sold digital deal jackets
from our platform into Tekion DMS, minimizing manual work.
Dealer Access to Sold Digital Deal Jackets:
The dealer accesses sold Digital Deal Jackets via Informativ’s Compliance Center.
Enter Tekion Deal Number:
The dealer enters the Tekion Deal Number in the designated field within the Deal
Jacket and saves changes.
Push Compliance Center-Generated Documents to Tekion DMS:
The entered Tekion deal number will initiate the push of the following Informativ
Compliance Center-generated documents to the Tekion DMS:
Deal documents
e-contracts of credit applications (e.g., contract documents)
e-contracts for F&I (e.g., contract documents)
Manually uploaded documents
Consumer identity documents (e.g., driver’s license details)
Note: No credit reports will be included in this process.
800.448.0183 hello@informativ.com informativ.com

chunks.filename: Informativ's RouteOne Integration Streamlines Compliance & Deal Submission with Digital Deal Jackets.pdf
chunks.content:

Streamline Compliance & Deal Submission with Digital Deal Jackets
Informativ’s integration with RouteOne simplifies and accelerates your data transfer
process and allows for instant updates whenever there are changes to an application,
keeping your data current and accurate. Most importantly, the integration ensures your
sales process remains compliant, reducing risk and providing peace of mind. The result:
faster deal funding, reduced errors, and greater compliance confidence.
How It Works
To submit directly to RouteOne in Informativ’s Compliance Center
(where all info needed for the sale is collected, created, and stored):
Click on the customer's name
Find “Customer Actions”
Select to “Send/Update lead to RouteOne”.
This action pushes the application to RouteOne’s Dashboard. The most recent credit
app is always listed at the top.
800.448.0183 hello@informativ.com informativ.com

Streamlined Lead Delivery from Driver’s License Scan to eLeads
Informativ’s seamless integration with CDK eLeads automates the capture, verification,
and delivery of high-quality leads directly into your dealership’s CRM. By combining
Driver’s License scanning and real-time fraud screening with instant lead submission to
eLeads, dealers reduce manual data entry, eliminate fraudulent inquiries, and accelerate
follow-up—all in one unified workflow.
Key Benefits

Scan a customer’s Driver’s License in seconds; pre-populated data flows instantly
into eLeads for immediate follow-up.

Real-time identity verification and fraud screening at the point of scan, reducing

ensures accurate, complete records.

Seamless handoff to eLeads, mapping all relevant fields.
