TOP DEALER PITFALLS
FROM FRAUD TO KICKED DEALS
Dealerships today operate in an environment where compliance, consumer privacy, and
operational consistency are no longer optional—they are essential. From decades working
with dealers, Informativ has distilled feedback and real-world incidents into the nine most
common and costly risks dealers face. Informativ helps dealers stay ahead of these risks by
reallocating “dead money” spent on credit into a platform that protects every deal, every day.
1 FRAUD
1 FRAUD
What’s happening now:
Dealers in our network have encountered 13,000+ fake IDs in just 18 months.
Fraud is growing—powered by unscrupulous actors with unlimited time,
unlimited budget, unlimited creativity, and generative AI that outpaces
manual checks.
Actionable insight:
13,000+
Use identity verification tools that compare IDs against multiple data
sources in real time.
Establish a tech enforced process for every buyer so that checks for ID,
unique fake IDs
synthetic, document, and internal fraud can’t be skipped.
in just 18 months
Check for fraud throughout the sales process – not just once.
Informativ’s edge:
Our platform’s multi-layered fraud detection ensures every ID is scanned
and every customer is validated to protect your profit while making your
sales process fast and seamless. Learn more here.
2 REMOTE DELIVERY
What’s happening now:
More and more deals are done remotely, where dealers lack physical presence, transparency, or control.
That creates opportunity for deception—“why do you want delivery here instead of at your home?” is a
question we now routinely ask.
Actionable insight:
Build remote delivery policies that require extra authentication or identity checks.
Log and audit all remote delivery requests.
Train staff to spot inconsistencies or evasive responses in delivery requests.
Informativ’s edge:
Whether the buyer is on the lot or across the country, our mobile and digital workflows maintain full
compliance and verification control.

3 PROCESS BREAKDOWN (“Policy Evaporation”)
What’s happening now:
Over time, dealership policies erode and training fades. Busy windows, employee turnover,
temptation—all lead to slipping standards.
Actionable insight:
Automate critical compliance steps so the process doesn’t rely on memory or manual
enforcement.
Use guardrails or checkpoints in your system to enforce policy.
Monitor adherence over time and flag deviations.
Informativ’s edge:
These automated guardrails guarantee consistent, compliant behavior —so even new or distracted
staff cannot “opt out” unintentionally. Learn more here.
4 PII ON PERSONAL DEVICES
What’s happening now:
Mobile phones are the tech of choice and convenient so it’s no
wonder we regularly see personal identifiable information
(driver’s licenses, pay stubs, insurance cards, etc.) stored
improperly on an employee’s personal device. Just one such $53,000
find poses a $53,000 liability for dealers.
Actionable insight: liability for a dealer
Disable or restrict download or screenshot capability of
sensitive documents in your systems.
Train staff explicitly to “never store PII on personal devices.”
Informativ’s edge:
Informativ replaces risky manual uploads with a secure mobile
app that automatically stores and encrypts customer data in a
digital deal jacket – to protect your dealership and customers.

5 PII LEFT IN THE SHOWROOM
What’s happening now:
Despite policies, “deal jackets” and paperwork are left out—sometimes right by the front door or
in collection bins just inside a glass door.
Actionable insight:
Enforce “no loose docs” policies with physical audits.
Use locked bins or secure document flows.
Rotate inspections during peak volume windows (e.g. last weekend of month).
Informativ’s edge:
Informativ not only eliminates paper risk – it builds efficiency by capturing and storing deal data
in digital deal jackets accessible to managers in real time which speeds up the sales process.
6 DEAD DEALS
What’s happening now:
Boxes of dead deals accumulate near exits or parking lot doors—untended and unsecured.
Actionable insight:
Define a strict, auditable retention process (e.g., scanned, archived).
Ensure dead deals are locked or removed daily.
Reduce dead deals by leveraging soft pulls to build deals. Only use hard credit pulls on sold deals.
Informativ’s edge:
Informativ stores dead deals electronically. You get FTC compliance, a full audit history, and fewer
dead deals by running hard pulls only when deals are finalized.
7 UNCONTROLLED EMPLOYEE ACCESS TO CUSTOMER INFO
What’s happening now:
Multiple lawsuits have resulted from employees manipulating or falsifying customer data.
Examples include $20M settlements in Illinois, $11M in Kansas, and a $7.2M judgment in
Arizona/New Mexico for falsified credit applications.
Actionable insight:
Implement role‐based access control so only necessary staff
can view/edit sensitive info.
Log every access, change, and deletion with timestamp and
user.
Run periodic audits comparing changes to supporting
documentation.
Informativ’s edge:
Informativ’s built‐in access controls and audit trails ensure any
data action is tracked and linked to a user with justification
required. You get visibility, accountability, and peace of mind.

Dealers juggle many compliance tasks: credit score impacts, FCRA consent, privacy statements,
risk‐based pricing notices, adverse action letters, and archiving for years. It’s a lot to manage.
Actionable insight:

Standardize templates and reduce manual variation.

CONCLUSION & NEXT STEPS
These nine risks are not theoretical. They are happening every day at dealerships nationwide and represent

Informativ’s approach is to bake in prevention, auditability, and automation directly into your workflows.
The goal: mitigate these risks without adding any burden to your staff.

chunks.filename: The Fraud Surge - What Dealers Need to Know With Informativ.pdf
chunks.content:
Synthetic and identity fraud are emerging as critical challenges for the automotive industry—
particularly for dealers. In the first half of 2024, one in 120 car loan applications contained
fraudulent information and 5% of all automotive transactions were fraudulent. Auto lending
fraud rose in 2024, up 98% from 2023, while synthetic fraud across all industries surged 35-
40% since 2019.
UNDERSTANDING SYNTHETIC FRAUD AND IDENTITY FRAUD
Synthetic Fraud
occurs when fraudsters create fictitious identities by blending real and
fabricated information, such as legitimate Social Security numbers with
false personal details. Another variation of synthetic fraud is the
Fraudsters can
combination of different identity elements from multiple people to form a
new fake identity. Unlike traditional identity theft, no specific individual's
easily produce fake
identity is stolen—fraudsters craft new ones.
driver’s licenses or
ID Fraud
signatures using
involves using counterfeit documents or stolen personal information to
deceive businesses or lenders into granting access to goods, services, or online AI tools for
financial products. Fraudsters can easily produce fake driver’s licenses or
as little as $1.50 per
signatures using online AI tools for as little as $1.50 per document .
document .
THE PERFECT STORM: WHY FRAUD IS SURGING
Several factors have contributed to the sharp rise in fraudulent activities within the auto industry:
COVID-19 Relief Program Expirations: During the pandemic, fraudsters exploited programs like the
Paycheck Protection Program (PPP) due to the lack of governance and oversight. As these
programs ended, scammers redirected their efforts to other vulnerable sectors, particularly the
automotive industry, with its high-value transactions and quick closing cycles.
Digital Retail and Online Transactions: As dealerships embrace digital retailing, the opportunities for
fraud multiply. Fraudsters take advantage of the anonymity of online deals, executing fraudulent
transactions without ever physically visiting a dealership .
AI Tools in Fraud: Fraudsters are increasingly using AI to create synthetic identities and fake
documents. In one case, it was revealed that AI tools could generate 1,000 fake documents in
minutes.

IMPACT ON DEALERSHIPS AND AUTO LENDERS
The repercussions of fraud are not only financial. Dealerships may face:
Losses in Revenue: A single fraudulent transaction involving a vehicle can
result in a dealership losing tens of thousands of dollars. One in 120 auto
loan applications was found to contain fraudulent synthetic identities .
In addition to the revenue loss—Jimmy Robinson, the Chief Financial Officer at Pohanka Auto Group,
adds that the time loss because of fraud for dealerships is substantial:
“
Before we implemented Informativ’s fraud detection software, we had six fraudulent car sales based
upon fake IDs in six months. We wasted a lot of time, resources, and focus from selling inventory to
chasing down the cars, doing damage control, working with insurance on claims. It’s painful. But
immediately after we put in the tech, the next 12 months we had zero fraudulent purchases that
went through. Fraud detection early in the process, rather than cleaning up after the fact, makes a
massive difference in the efficiency of our stores and salespeople”.
Jimmy Robinson, CFO, Pohanka Auto Group
Damage to Brand Reputation: A dealership's credibility can be severely tarnished by association
with fraud, leading to lost consumer trust. An estimated 40% of consumers report awareness of a
data breach will affect their purchasing decisions .
Risking Lender Relationships: In addition to the damage to a dealer’s reputation, fraud-related
breaches erode trust between auto dealerships and lenders by exposing them to higher risk and
financial losses. This can lead to stricter lending terms, increased scrutiny, and resource-consuming
audits, ultimately harming its ability to offer competitive financing.
Operational Risk: The average dealership detects only 19.5% of fraud attempts, leaving them highly
vulnerable . This underscores the urgent need for better fraud detection mechanisms throughout
the transaction process, from pre-qualification to final deal closure.
Jesse Cordaway, Director of Operations at Parks Automotive Group, emphasizes his personal
experience of realizing how little fraud his dealership group had been detecting before incorporating
fraud detection tools:
“
You don't realize how many fraud issues you have until you have a technology in place. We had a
customer in the dealership who had a driver’s license that, to all the sales personnel, looked and
seemed perfect—driver's license number, date of birth, address, you name it. Luckily for us, we ran it
through Informativ's compliance app and detected the fraud before we delivered a $100,000+ Dodge
Demon. Building this technology into every step of our sales has changed everything. Enforcing the
process, the compliance, and the fraud detection with tech has made us realize how much we were
missing beforehand.”
Jesse Cordaway, Director of Operations
Parks Automotive Group

chunks.content:
COMPLIANCE VIOLATIONS
5
PUTTING YOUR
DEALERSHIP AT RISK
The expanded FTC Safeguards Rule took effect June 9, 2023. The compliance requirements can have a
huge impact on your dealership if you weren't prepared and will result in devastating fines. Here are
five of the top violations that put your dealership at risk and how leading dealers avoid them.
VIOLATION 1:
Not having a way to enforce a consistent, compliant process on every single deal. The saying is ‘hope
is not a strategy’, but that’s what many dealers rely on when it comes to compliance. Whether you have
a busy dealership, staff turnover or haven’t consistently trained your staff, your dealership needs an
enforceable physical and administrative compliance process that leverages technology to ensure your
dealership is compliant. Bonus points if the solution (like ours) is one that your team will love and will
help them sell cars faster.
VIOLATION 2:
Sales staff storing consumer information on their cell phone or personal email. Really, it’s not just
sales staff. At a recent NADA Show, a GM confessed to having 100s of driver’s license photos on his
phone. This seemingly “easy” way to capture the info is extremely costly. By using a solution with an
app (like ours), you can quickly capture the driver’s license info AND check for fraud.
VIOLATION 3:
Not securely sending and receiving consumer information for remote deals. Instead of having buyers
email or text information to your staff - - which is not compliant - - send them a secure link (like our tech
has) that allows them to upload the documents you need to process the sale.
VIOLATION 4:
Private consumer information left exposed in unsecure locations. Think – salesperson’s desk, the copy
machine. All of this exposed personal information leaves you at risk for violations and hefty fines.
Leveraging a platform (like ours) that almost completely eliminates paper protects your dealership and
your customers.
VIOLATION 5:
Not storing dead deals for the required 5 years. That’s a lot of deals with lots of personal information
that you’re responsible for storing, protecting, and PROVING that you’re retaining. Our solution
stores the dead deal jackets electronically and supports your compliance checks.
WANT TO LEARN HOW TOP DEALERS MANAGE HUNDREDS OF
VEHICLE SALES EVERY MONTH?
Contact us today for a free 10-minute demo at informativ.com/get-a-demo.

chunks.source: Informativ Payments — AI Discoverability and Structured Payment Data
chunks.content:
Informativ Payments — Why Structured Payment Data Determines Which Dealers AI Agents Discover

THE BIG SHIFT:
Car buyers are no longer starting their research with Google. Increasingly, they're asking AI assistants like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Mode. Instead of clicking links, buyers now receive direct answers from AI. Dealerships without machine-readable data simply won't be cited by AI agents. Most of the automotive technology stack isn't built for that shift yet.

THE NEW SEARCH STACK — SEO WAS JUST THE BEGINNING:
SEO (Search Engine Optimization) focused on keyword density, backlinks, page authority. AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) shifted the goal to appearing in featured snippets and AI summaries. GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) — now the currency isn't a click, it's a citation inside an AI-generated answer.

Buyers increasingly ask AI questions like: 'What's the monthly payment on a 2025 CR-V with a 680 credit score and $3,000 down in Austin?' For most dealers, AI can't answer that question using their data. It responds with a competitor's content, a generic estimate, or nothing at all. That's not a content gap — that's a revenue gap.

KEY MARKET SIGNALS:
- 25% predicted drop in traditional search volume by 2026 (Gartner)
- 4.4x higher conversion rates from AI search vs traditional organic search (SEMRush)
- 93% zero-click rate in Google's AI Mode
- 50% of searches expected to involve AI assistants by 2028 (Gartner)
- 528% growth in AI platform traffic in 2025 (Previsible)

WHY STRUCTURED PAYMENT DATA IS THE KEY:
AI agents are drawn to data that is specific, verifiable, locally-grounded, and fresh. Generic content fails all four tests. A real, credit-qualified, VIN-level monthly payment with live OEM incentives, local taxes, and dealer fees passes all four. Content with specific, verifiable facts improves AI citation rates by 30-40%. A payment in Phoenix is genuinely different from one in Philadelphia — different taxes, dealer fees, regional OEM incentives. That hyper-locality is a GEO signal.

chunks.source: Informativ Payments — Platform Implications for AI Discoverability
chunks.content:
Informativ Payments — What Structured Payment Data Means for Every Platform Serving Dealers

Every platform needs to ask: Can an AI agent discover, parse, and cite the data we provide on behalf of dealers?

DEALER WEBSITE PLATFORMS: Payment-enriched VDPs, geo-targeted landing pages, and credit-tier content become AI-citable assets. Platforms that embed live payment data surface in AI answers; those that don't lose organic discovery as AI Overviews replace blue links.

DMS PROVIDERS: The DMS holds ground truth on inventory, pricing, and deal structure. A DMS with Payments API integration becomes an AI-native platform. One without it becomes a legacy back-office system.

MARKETPLACES & AGGREGATORS: Cars.com, AutoTrader, TrueCar — listings enriched with live, VIN-level payment data are dramatically more likely to surface in AI-generated shopping summaries than sticker-price-only listings.

CRMs & LEAD PLATFORMS: When an AI agent sends a pre-qualified buyer already knowing their credit range and preferred payment, the CRM must respond with verified payment intelligence — not a generic callback. Structured payment data closes the loop.

AI-NATIVE DEALER ASSISTANTS: The next generation of dealer AI isn't a chatbot — it's an agent that negotiates, pre-qualifies, and structures deals in real time. Real, credit-qualified, incentive-aware payments are the transactability layer that makes it worth trusting.

AGENCIES & SEO PARTNERS: Payment Content SEO is an entirely new service line. Geo-targeted payment pages, credit tier guides, and OEM incentive explainers built on live API data cannot be replicated without the underlying feed — a client retention tool that compounds as AI search scales.

THE FLYWHEEL: AI citation authority compounds. Platforms that establish structured, verifiable, locally-grounded payment content early will build citation dominance that new entrants cannot easily replicate. This is not a feature — it's a strategic moat.

chunks.title: The Fraud Surge — Synthetic and Identity Fraud (Whitepaper Summary)
chunks.content:
THE FRAUD SURGE — Understanding Synthetic Fraud and Identity Fraud in Auto Retail

CORE INDUSTRY DATA (use these stats in fraud conversations):
- 1 in 120 car loan applications contained fraudulent information in the first half of 2024
- 5% of all automotive transactions were fraudulent in 2024
- Auto lending fraud rose 98% in 2024 vs 2023
- Synthetic fraud across all industries surged 35-40% since 2019
- Fraudsters can produce fake driver's licenses or signatures using online AI tools for as little as $1.50 per document
- AI tools can generate 1,000 fake documents in minutes
- The average dealership detects only 19.5% of fraud attempts
- 40% of consumers say awareness of a data breach will affect their purchasing decisions

DEFINITIONS:
- Synthetic Fraud: fictitious identities created by blending real and fabricated information (e.g., legitimate SSNs with false personal details), or combining identity elements from multiple people to form a new fake identity. No specific individual's identity is stolen.
- ID Fraud: counterfeit documents or stolen personal information used to deceive businesses or lenders.

WHY FRAUD IS SURGING IN AUTO:
1. COVID-19 relief program expirations pushed fraudsters into other vulnerable sectors
2. Digital retail and online transactions multiply the opportunities (anonymity of online deals)
3. AI tools enable mass-production of synthetic identities and fake documents

IMPACT ON DEALERSHIPS:
- Revenue Losses: a single fraudulent transaction = tens of thousands of dollars lost
- Damage to Brand Reputation
- Risking Lender Relationships (stricter terms, increased scrutiny, audits)
- Operational Risk: average dealer detects only 19.5% of attempts

CUSTOMER VOICES (use for fraud narratives):
- Jimmy Robinson, CFO, Pohanka Auto Group: "Before we implemented Informativ's fraud detection software, we had six fraudulent car sales based upon fake IDs in six months. We wasted a lot of time, resources, and focus from selling inventory to chasing down the cars, doing damage control, working with insurance on claims. It's painful. But immediately after we put in the tech, the next 12 months we had zero fraudulent purchases that went through."
- Jesse Cordaway, Director of Operations, Parks Automotive Group: "You don't realize how many fraud issues you have until you have a technology in place. We had a customer in the dealership who had a driver's license that, to all the sales personnel, looked and seemed perfect. Luckily, we ran it through Informativ's compliance app and detected the fraud before we delivered a $100,000+ Dodge Demon."
- Ben Stillwagon, Director of IT, Cavender Auto Family: "There was a couple at one of our stores trying to steal two G-wagons — very expensive, $200,000 cars. When we scanned their driver's license, they were immediately flagged as fraudulent. Cops took them away in handcuffs. We wouldn't have caught them without Informativ's driver's license fraud detection tech."

FRAUD EXAMPLES TO REFERENCE:
- Car Stacking: synthetic identities used to purchase multiple vehicles from different dealerships within a short timeframe before fraud is detected
- Test Drive Scams: fake IDs used to take vehicles for "test drives" that never return

STRATEGIC MEASURES (Informativ's positioning):
1. Synthetic Fraud Detection Tools — device ID checks, synthetic ID alerts at the BEGINNING of the sales process
2. Multi-layered ID Verification — driver's license scanners with real-time counterfeit detection
3. Biometric Verification at closing
4. Employee Training (Informativ supports but doesn't replace)
5. Consistent Fraud Prevention Across Channels (in-person AND online)
6. Vendor Collaboration — work with holistic fraud prevention vendors, not piecemeal point solutions

USE THIS FOR:
- Discovery calls when you suspect fraud is an underweighted concern
- ROI conversations (one prevented fraud > annual subscription cost typically)
- Compliance Officer persona — they live this data daily
- Pre-meeting prep on dealers known to have had a fraud incident

# Sales Pipeline Management Rules & Guidelines — 2026

Purpose: To establish clear rules and guidelines for managing sales opportunities in alignment with territory assignments, ensuring fair practices and efficient use of resources. These rules are calibrated to align with the company’s pipeline hygiene and forecasting models, which serve as the systems of record for pipeline health scoring and monthly revenue forecasting.
Company Discretion Clause: The company reserves the right to adjust, modify, or make exceptions to these guidelines at its sole discretion. Any adjustments or exceptions will be made in consideration of business needs, organizational priorities, or unique circumstances and will be communicated to the relevant parties as appropriate.
Pipeline Rules
Pipeline Ownership Timeline:
Opportunities must demonstrate active engagement and stage progression within the following stage-specific thresholds to remain in the sales rep’s pipeline. These thresholds are calibrated from 1.5× the recent four-quarter average stage duration from the Stage Duration Report and are the values used by the pipeline hygiene model:
Solution Fit: flag if age exceeds 120 days (1.5× recent 4Q avg of 80 days)
Tech Demo: flag if age exceeds 145 days (1.5× recent 4Q avg of 96 days)
Proposal: flag if age exceeds 100 days (1.5× recent 4Q avg of 65 days)
Committed: flag if age exceeds 40 days (1.5× recent 4Q avg of 26 days)
Active engagement is defined as documented activity (calls, emails, meetings) logged in Salesforce. Mass marketing emails do not qualify. Thresholds will be reviewed and updated quarterly as new Stage Duration data becomes available.
By the 5th business day of each month, Managers and RSM’s will conduct Pipeline Review meetings using the monthly Pipeline Hygiene Report generated by the hygiene model. During those meetings it will be up to the RSM to demonstrate all opportunities are valid and still in flight with active engagement.
Failure to demonstrate that an opportunity is progressing forward will result in closing of the opportunity as part of the pipeline clean-up process.
The closed opportunity will be labeled as Pipeline hygiene by the RSM/manager and closed out unless there is a more fitting closed/lost reason.
Criteria for Active Opportunities:
An opportunity is considered active if there has been documented activity (e.g., calls, emails, meetings) within the stage-specific age thresholds defined above.
Reps must update opportunities with:
Stage progression.
Relevant notes in Salesforce.
A valid deal amount that meets the product minimum: Full Bundled Solution $15,000 minimum; CBC $1,200 minimum; CBC + CreditDriver $12,000 minimum; DSGSS $2,400 minimum. Amounts of $0, null, or below the product minimum will be flagged by the hygiene model as a data quality issue.
An accurate close date. Opportunities with a close date in a month that has already passed will be flagged as overdue by the hygiene model.
The RSM’s will utilize the Kanban view of My Opportunities and clear all alerts on a weekly basis.
Alerts identify Opportunities with no follow-up tasks or overdue tasks.
Failure to maintain a clean pipeline may result in opportunities being closed by management after the monthly Pipeline Review meetings.
For all disqualified opportunities the RSM will create follow-up tasks on all appropriate contacts.
Activity and scheduled follow-ups on the contact record will be utilized to continue the prospecting cycle if applicable.
Example: Kanban View of My Opportunities with alert identified.
Inactive Opportunities:
Opportunities exceeding the stage-specific age thresholds above, or with missing/invalid amounts or overdue close dates, will be flagged automatically by the Pipeline Hygiene Report and surfaced as At-Risk for review during the Pipeline Review Meetings.
Validation of Opportunity Stage:
Managers must validate the stage of the opportunity (e.g., Solution FIt vs. Proposal) before approving pipeline retention during territory shifts.
Territory Realignment Transition Process
Initial Assessment:
Managers will review all open opportunities in Salesforce prior to realignment.
Sales reps must present an updated status for each deal, including expected close dates and key next steps.
Retention of Later-Stage Opportunities:
Later-stage deals (Tech Demo Completed or beyond) may remain with the original rep for up to 90 days to ensure continuity and a smooth close.
Aging opportunities with no active engagement (defined above) will not be eligible for retention.
Managers will oversee handoff timelines for any deals that remain open beyond 90 days.
Handoff of Early-Stage Opportunities:
Early-stage deals must be handed off to the new territory owner, with detailed notes entered into Salesforce.
The original rep must participate in a handoff meeting with the new owner to ensure a seamless transition.
At the Reps discretion they can negotiate a split commission to ensure the deal closes.  All commission splits are the responsibility of the reps involved and must be approved my management.
Support for Transitioning Reps:
Managers will work with impacted reps to develop a priority prospecting list for their new territory.
The Sales Development Representative (SDR) team will prioritize these accounts to rebuild pipelines.
It is the Reps responsibility to develop the list and to work with their manager to ensure the list is sent to the SDR program.
Territory Shifts and Deal Ownership:
Reps may retain later-stage opportunities (Tech Demo, Proposal or Contract stages) in their pipeline for up to 90 days following a territory realignment.
Early-stage opportunities (Solution Fit) must be handed off to the new territory owner upon realignment.
Monitoring and Reporting
Salesforce Hygiene Checks:
Sales Operations will conduct periodic reviews to ensure all opportunities meet activity requirements.
Opportunities flagged for inactivity will be escalated to Regional Sales Managers (RSMs) and Director of Sales for review and resolution in their monthly pipeline review calls.
Pipeline Transition Dashboard:
A shared dashboard will be developed so that all parties can track:
Opportunities retained by reps post-realignment.
Opportunities that have an agreed upon commission split
Accountability Metrics:
Reps are expected to maintain 80% pipeline integrity, defined as no more than 20% of their deals flagged by the monthly Pipeline Hygiene Report. A flagged deal is any opportunity with: (1) age exceeding the stage-specific threshold, (2) a missing or below-minimum deal amount, or (3) an overdue close date. The hygiene rate is calculated and reported per rep each month in the “By Rep” tab of the Hygiene Report
Forecast Model Alignment
Monthly Forecast: By the 5th business day of each month, management will re-run the forecast against the current pipeline export and distribute the forecast summary to sales leadership. The forecast model applies stage win rates, product multipliers, deal age factors, and close-date timing factors to produce a weighted forecast with conservative and optimistic ranges.
Monthly Hygiene Report: The Pipeline Hygiene Report will be run concurrently with the forecast and distributed to managers ahead of Pipeline Review meetings. The report includes a Summary tab, a full Flagged Deals tab sorted by severity, and a By Rep tab for individual accountability conversations.
Model Recalibration: After each month closes, actual bookings will be logged in the History tab of the forecast report. The stage win rates and thresholds will be reviewed quarterly and updated in the scripts to reflect the most recent Stage Duration Report data. Significant deviations between forecast and actual (greater than ±20%) will trigger a model review.
Enforcement
Non-compliance with pipeline management guidelines will result in escalation to the Chief Revenue Officer.
Persistent issues may result in formal performance reviews.
Benefits of These Guidelines
Fairness: Prevents reps from unfairly “claiming” deals while allowing continuity for later-stage opportunities.
Accountability: Encourages regular updates and activity logging in Salesforce to keep the pipeline clean.
Efficiency: Facilitates smooth transitions during territory shifts, maintaining momentum and avoiding customer confusion.
Growth: Supports transitioning reps by prioritizing their new territories for SDR outreach and prospecting.
Accuracy: clean pipeline data directly improves the accuracy of the monthly forecast model, which has been calibrated to a 31.4% CRM-to-actual realization rate. Every corrected deal amount, updated close date, and advanced stage improves forecast precision

# DID NOT MATERIALIZE - STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE


#### Document Control

SOP Number: OPS | 240125
Effective Date: 01/21/2025
Revision Number: 3
Last Revised: 01/21/2025
Review Date: [02/18/2025]
Approved By: Kimberly Lang, Chief Operating Officer
Prepared By: Emily Milutin, Director of Client Operations


### 1. Purpose

This document outlines a standardized process for managing Did Not Materialize (DNM) cases, with the primary goal of re-engaging customers and driving timely implementations. It clarifies roles and responsibilities, defines escalation paths, and establishes resolution procedures to ensure accountability, transparency, and a consistent approach to overcoming implementation barriers.
This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) applies to all Closed-Won bookings that are at risk of being classified as Did Not Materialize (DNM). It requires coordinated efforts between the Implementation Team, Regional Sales Managers (RSMs), and leadership stakeholders to re-engage customers and ensure successful, timely implementations.
This SOP covers:
Initial Client Engagement Protocols: Clear steps for the first attempts to re-engage with the client.
Defined Timelines and Responsibilities: Specific expectations for both the Implementation Team and RSMs to maintain momentum.
Escalation Procedures: Structured escalation paths for unresolved cases, including criteria for leadership involvement and final decision-making.
2. Responsibilities
Implementation Team:
Client Engagement Primary Responsibility: Initiate and lead client engagement immediately upon project receipt.
Preferred Contact Method: Prioritize phone calls over emails to improve response rates, with emails serving as supplementary follow-ups.
Engagement Process: Execute the CE3 Process—a combination of calls and emails 3 times per week—for an initial two-week period.
Early Escalation: If no meaningful contact is established within the first two weeks or for any two-week period during the implementation process, escalate to both the Director of Client Operations and the assigned RSM to support re-engagement efforts.
Ongoing Collaboration: Continue collaborating with the RSM throughout the engagement period, not just after escalation.
Deactivation: Ensure all systems are deactivated for accounts officially classified as DNM after all escalation protocols have been exhausted.
Regional Sales Manager (RSM):
Transition: Provide an overview of the key stakeholders to implementations with guidance on successful communication.
Client Hand-off: Attending the Implementation Kick-Off call to conduct introductions with the client and implementations to ensure a smooth handover.
Early Involvement: Actively engage with the Implementation Team if contact attempts are unsuccessful.
Support Role: Assist in customer re-engagement efforts through direct outreach, leveraging existing relationships to re-establish communication.
Ownership After Escalation: If the customer remains unresponsive for four weeks assume primary responsibility for continued engagement.
Progress Updates: Provide weekly updates to the Implementation Team and the Director of Client Operations on client status and engagement efforts.
Escalation Path: Escalate unresolved cases to Sales Leadership when necessary.
Director of Client Operations:
Active Engagement: Play an active role in re-engagement efforts, not just oversight. Directly contact customers when escalated by the Implementation Team, working alongside the RSM.
Decision Authority: Review and approve DNM status classifications to ensure all re-engagement avenues have been exhausted.
Escalation Management: Escalate high-risk DNM customers to Executive Level Leadership (ELT) when critical accounts are at risk.
Cross-Functional Liaison: Engage with Sales Leadership proactively for cases showing early signs of disengagement, supporting both the Implementation Team and RSMs in recovery efforts.
Sales Leadership Team:
Strategic Support: Address DNM risks during the sales team weekly sales standup, providing strategic guidance and recommendations for re-engagement.
Direct Client Involvement: Step in to support high-priority cases where both the Implementation Team and RSM have been unable to re-engage the customer within the specified timeframe.
Leadership Escalation: Engage directly with key accounts if escalated by the Director of Client Operations for critical intervention.
Client On-site visit: When appropriate coordinate a site visit with the salesperson as a last-ditch effort to reengage the customer.


### 3. Definitions

4. Definitions
DNM (Did Not Materialize): A status assigned to a Closed Won booking that cannot be implemented or installed.
CE3 Process: A structured engagement approach prioritizing phone contact, involving three phone calls per week, supplemented by emails as necessary,
On Hold with Sub-Status DNM Risk: A temporary status assigned when initial engagement attempts fail, indicating the project is at risk of becoming a DNM. Deals can remain in this status for up to 6 months, after which bookings will be removed and the account will require recredentialing to move forward.
Closed Won Booking: A sale recorded as successfully closed but not yet implemented.

### 4. Procedure

Process for Managing Did Not Materialize (DNM) Cases
Initial Engagement:
Upon receiving a project, the Implementation Team initiates client engagement efforts using the CE3 process (three phone calls per week, supplemented by emails).
The RSM is kept informed of progress during this initial engagement period.
Escalation to RSM Collaboration:
If client engagement fails after a two-week period, the RSM is formally notified.
The RSM and the Implementation Team work collaboratively over the next two weeks to re-engage the customer, utilizing both direct outreach and strategic follow-ups.
All efforts made by the RSM and the Implementation team must be logged into the company's CRM.
Escalation to On Hold (DNM Risk Status):
If re-engagement efforts are unsuccessful after the abovementioned four-week period, the project is placed On Hold with the sub-status of DNM Risk.
The RSM assumes full responsibility for continued client follow-up.
Sales Leadership is notified by the Implementation Team of the DNM Risk status and will be required to report on all DNM deals during weekly sales meetings.
Sales Leadership Engagement Period:
Sales Leadership has five months to support re-engagement efforts, which may include:
Strategic client outreach
Direct involvement from leadership
Potential on-site visits by the RSM and manager, if deemed necessary
Final Decision:
At the conclusion of the six-month period or if determined sooner by Sales Leadership, if the customer remains unresponsive:
The project will be officially placed in DNM status.
All bookings associated with the DNM rooftop will be removed.
All commissions paid related to the DNM deal will be clawed back.
Post-DNM Actions:
The Implementation Team ensures all systems are deactivated if they have been activated.
The Director of Client Operations sets the project status to DNM and selects the appropriate DNM reason.
If the client re-engages after being marked as DNM:
The project must restart through the sales process, including the execution of a new contract.
Commissions will be re-evaluated based on the new sale.
Escalation and Reporting:
The Director of Client Operations escalates high-risk DNM cases to the Executive Leadership Team (ELT) as needed.
DNM risks are highlighted during weekly sales meetings to ensure ongoing visibility among the RSM group and Sales Leadership.


### 5. Exceptions

If a customer is not able to be implemented due to compliance, lack of permissible purpose the project will be marked DNM with the appropriate reason once the reason is identified, documented, and confirmed by the Compliance Manager in consultation with the Director of Client Operations, Sales Leadership, and/or an Informativ Executive.
The Sales Leadership team will be responsible for raising these instances at the weekly sales meeting.
Additional exceptions to the outlined timelines or procedures can be made by the Director of Client Operations or any Informativ Executive. These decisions will be documented and communicated to all stakeholders.
Managing Inability to Exit Third-Party Contracts During Implementation
If a customer notifies Informativ during the implementation process that they are unable to exit a third-party contract, the following steps will be taken:
The Implementation Team will notify the assigned RSM immediately.
The RSM will engage with the customer to re-contract Total Solutions Lite.
A new implementation project will be created once all necessary documents have been signed.
The old project will be canceled.


### 6. Records

DNM Dashboard: DNM Dashboard | Salesforce
Sales Standup Dashboard: Imp.Sales Standup Dashboard | Salesforce

David’s track for the new deck from August RSM training
I would like to thank you guys for giving us some of your time. Having been in a dealership before, I know how hard it is to break free many times so I am very appreciative.
So on my screen right now and you can see my screen right?
Yes, yes.
You're probably seeing “Powered by Informative” at the bottom there. That's our company or brand if you will. And you may not have heard of Informative, but what you probably have heard of are the brands that make up Informative. We've done something unique in the industry. We have our credit reselling tool, CBC or credit Bureau connection, which has been around for years. The other side there, we have a very new, unique and disruptive instant prequal tool/marketing supplement tool. You can use this in a myriad of ways.
It's called Credit Driver. We'll get into that a little bit, but in the middle, tying both together is dealer Safeguard Solutions, which is really like the linchpin of our program. It's our compliance and process enforcement platform.
What we've done that's so unique in the industry is we've combined all three of those products to create the industry's first and only whole credit and compliance suite. So you are compliantly covered from lead to sale with credit included. In other words, we are generally able to provide our partner dealers these solutions for the price they are paying only for credit.
We'll get into how we do that, how we set that up.
What we've really done, and what we have set out to do in the market is create a solution that covers you from your showroom to your desk to the F and I department, and even as far as accounting, a compliant and secure solution for all customer information.
From a showroom perspective, we're going to eliminate paperwork in the showroom helping automate and digitize documents that have historically been paper.
We do this by helping you with the data that you create and collect by scanning the customer’s ID putting it directly into the CRM, so there's not a duplication of efforts on your salesperson’s part. We're going to give them a quick and easy process where they're going to follow step one, Step 2, step 3 every time, every single day. Whether it's a busy Saturday afternoon or slow on a Tuesday night, your team is going to follow the exact same process, YOUR process every time, regardless of lead source.
Your desk managers are going to have real time, at a glance, insight to everything going on in the show room. Really what we want to do is put your showroom on autopilot.
They're truly going to be able to go into that dashboard and say, I know that I had a customer come in at this time, they're working with this salesperson. They will be able to see how the deal has advanced. For example, we have scanned their license, we've gotten as far as a privacy notice. At a glance the desk will be able to see the progress that is updated automatically.
And then in the middle of doing credit,
it literally is at a glance control of the showroom. Every single one of those documents that's either scanned in or loaded into the system is visible and updated to the manager dashboard in real time.
From there they will be able to access soft pulls and hard pulls from here. We have deal portal submission integration, so there's not any duplication of efforts.
In the event a fraudulent license is scanned, they're going to be notified first, instantaneously, in real time, and we're going to highlight that result in red on the dashboard so it cannot be missed. We'll send emails to everybody you need us, including your other rooftops if you have any, to ensure everyone is in the know there is a fraud suspect at large.
At the F&I level, like I said before, we've got full integration into route one and dealer track. So you'll under submission tools are covered. They will be able to view and print any document they need for wet signatures like state specific documents, things like that. Because we exist ahead of the contracting process, there is no change to your contracting process. There are no changes needed to your F and I menus.
We do have a tool that allows you to capture any stip you need from the customer, whether they're in front of you or not, in a digital compliant manner.
We can even take this as far as the accounting office and provide them with the same manager level access. They'll be able to view documents, print the jacket for cold storage, export things to digital cold storage. We can set up workflows for them. And as with anyone in the store, everybody manager level access can view all that deal jackets going back as far as you want. So from the showroom to the desk, from F and I and accounting, it really is a new compliant process.
Now this solution was designed integrate into your current workflows. Our goal is never to blow up your sales process, but rather help enforce it. That's what makes you successful.
The only time, in fact, that we make crazy recommendations on a, on a drastic shift in process is that what you're doing today is so out of compliance that we're “OK, You know that isn’t the best practice; You may want to change that.”
But really what we try to do is build this software so that it acts as what we like to call a better mouse trap.
OK. And the easiest way to show you what this could look like, and I say could because you know look everything's configurable. Let's go through a basic Road to the sale. If we had a walk-in leak, that's going to be the most common. Now like I said before, this works regardless of lease lead source, but this is going to be the easiest way to show you the flow, OK?
So we're going to go ahead and imagine that Trish's might customer and I'm a sales Rep at your dealership, right? Trish walks in the door. I'm going to do all this stuff that you taught me to do. I'm going to do a proper greeting at the door. I might do a proper needs assessment. I'm going to land her on a car and do a walk around, and low and behold, Trish likes the ca, she likes the car, she likes me. She wants to do a test drive at that stage of the game.
That is when our solution kind of takes off. I'm going to pull out my phone; There’s no need for me to take the license and walk away from her and disconnect, I'm going to pull out my phone and log in with my own username and password because there's always a record of who does what and when they do it. There's always a forensic paper trail, if you will.
And when I log into that app, this is the informative compliance app.
(It's going to walk me through quickly and easily. I'm going to capture the license and the way I do that is I just simply do with the app tells me to do I'm going to hit click scan DL button.
It's going to take me into the next screen and it's going to walk me through the process. I'm going to scan the top of the license, scan the frontal license, then the back of the license where the barcode sits, and I'm going to pull that information up on the screen to verify at this stage of the game, if the customer’s address is correct or if it needs to be updated, we can edit that. We can require that your sales Rep gets the mobile number and e-mail or both at this time if you wish, because remember, this is going to go to the CRM automatically. So would be a good idea to verify their contact data.
And as soon as I'm done, I'm simply going to click upload customer, create buyer. And when I do that, the software is doing a few things in the background. First things first, it's capturing a perfect image of that license and it's taking the data from that license, the pertinent customer contact data, and it's shooting it over the CRM as a lead in the salesperson's name, number one.  Then number two, it is measuring that license for fraud. Now we've got proprietary technology that's very similar to what you find at TSA when you check in at the airport, it accounts
for all 250 different variations of driver's licenses being issued across the different states right now and we go as granular as the barcode within the barcode.
(Maybe you know, or maybe you didn't, there's a bar code on the back of a license that contains the public data which most software can read. There's also a hidden barcode within that barcode that is specific to this customer, and it is extremely difficult to replicate, it is essentially impossible to mimic or defraud. That's how granular we go.
So with a 99.99% accuracy, we are going to be able to tell you if that license was fake.
In fact, we caught over 5500 fraudulent attempts last year alone, saving our dealers cumulatively potential losses of over $100 million.
It is the best in the business. And I don't say that because I'm biased, I say that because it costs us a ton of money to scrutinize the bar codes to the degree we do.
Now, what happens when you scan a license? When you scan a license, in any case, it is going to create a digital deal jacket for the manager. So Trish is my customer. I scanned her license and instantaneously the manager at the desk now sees there's been a new digital deal jacket has created with Trisha's name on it.
And as you can see, the license column is going to turn green. Signifying to the manager that Trish is a walk in lead. I've talked to her. She came in at this time and we scanned her ID.
Now if Tricia's license was fraudulent, we're going to email everyone on your distro list and turn that row in the dashboard red. So it is nice and early in the process, you will know if you have somebody trying to take you for a ride here, because we have determined that it's a fraudulent ID. By design we only notify the managers there is a fraudulent ID detected because we don't want the sales Rep being accusatory or making the decisions on what the next steps are going to be.
But if that license is fake, we notify the right people in the store.
Now, I know Trish is not a fraudster, right? So what I've done here is I've basically scanned the license, I've created a digital deal jacket, and the manager now knows that I'm working with her and I scanned in the license. We've got the first part of our deal jacket, whether it's a sold deal or a dead deal, it's now created.
OK, so back to our little example here. Trish and I go on a test drive, and we get back to the dealership and I start the trial closes. I ask Trish if she likes the car.
You know what? I love the car.
So there are a few ways you could go with this do it right, One way is to let her know “we take protecting your credit and identity here very seriously, but I can get you some very accurate numbers together with 0 impact on your credit score. Is that something you'd want to do? Trish says yes, so I can have her scan a QR code on the back of my business card, or I can send a link from my phone to her phone, and I can guide her out to this dedicated co-branded landing page, with just the information you want on it.
This is our Customer Insights tool, formerly known as CreditDriver, and all the customer needs to do is plug in their phone number, and when they do that, our technology goes to work. A waterfall detection process in the background determines if it is the actual user or if someone else might be using the device like someone in a family plan. Once we verify her identity, we ping the SIM card of that device, verify her identity with her mobile service carrier, and we receive Tricia's NPI, all of it, and proceed for her to enter 3 more bits of information to finish the informed consent for a soft pull.
Bottom line, we're vetting this person with another fraud detection tool.
Moving forward with your process, we stay on the license at the beginning. We now do the credit driver link, so once she goes through and gives us permission to access her credit header, it prefills the pre qual app for her, so now all she has to do is click confirm. We've now done a synthetic ID fraud check, vetted Trish as a real person with a valid driver’s license, and she's not a synthetic ID fraudster. Multiple layers of protection and multiple instances of automatic data collection for your team.
Once we have her permission, that pre qual application shoots into our compliance program as an informed consent application, and that gives the desk manager an instant consent to do all three bureau soft pulls on Trish here for Informativ. This really changes the game, especially since our soft pulls are mirror images of the hard pull.
As you can see, you literally get all of the data on a soft pull that you would get on a hard pull. I remember soft pulls back in the day kind of got a bad rap, right? Because you would do a soft pull and it would give you a score. So you get a 750 score, but the data was limited. We get excited, we think I'm going to put a deal together and you would work the deal, agree to figures and lo and behold, the poor guy, just went through bankruptcy. There might have been a hundred point score swing between soft pull and hard pull. That is no longer the case.
This is a soft pull example with us and as you can see this customer is obviously a fake person, but John consumer here is a 674. We see this as an Equifax credit prequal and look at the data we provide you: Here is the real Bureau. Overview
Carmatic's dealer-facing solution, powered by an API suite, offers a shop-by-payment experience with 1,400 cars. It allows dealers to configure payments, including lease and loan deals, and consider factors like profit and equity. The system optimizes payments by crunching various permutations and applying rules specific to lenders. It also maximizes dealer profit by considering front-end and back-end gross. Carmatic handles tax, title, and license fees, and it updates jurisdiction-based incentives and fees. The system includes a recommendation API for profit-based vehicle suggestions, enhancing deal efficiency.
Outline
Shop by Payment Experience Overview
Speaker introduces the dealer-facing solution powered by the API suite, highlighting the shop by payment experience.
The system currently has 1400 cars across multiple makes and models, allowing users to filter results based on payment amounts.
An example is given where the system filters results to show cars with payments between $450 to $550 per month.
The system also allows filtering based on features like Apple CarPlay, best lease deals, best loan deals, highest age inventory, and profit.
Dealer Configuration and Payment Engine
Speaker 1 explains that dealers configure their preferred lenders and deal structures, including taxes, titles, licenses, fees, and markups.
The payment engine considers various factors like guaranteed consumer cash and profit from both front-end and back-end gross.
In this instance, the dealer has chosen to start payments with a $2,500 down payment and excellent credit score, considering different lease and loan permutations.
Carmatic crunches numbers across various permutations to find the best possible payment for leases and loans.
Handling Equity and Deal Structure
Speaker 1 discusses how Carmatic handles equity with down payments, updating numbers to reflect the equity brought in.
The system helps consumers narrow down inventory results and can be used in consumer-facing experiences like marketplaces or dealer websites.
The majority of experiences today use estimated payments, but Carmatic offers real transactional payments that consider the full deal structure.
Speaker 1 selects a vehicle to show the payment display page, highlighting the best possible payment for a 10,000-mile 36-month lease provided by Hyundai Motor Finance.
Lease and Loan Details
The lease deal is structured with tax, title, license fees, and the first month's payment due at signing, but this is configurable.
The best possible loan under the given parameters is a 72-month dealer choice APR provided by Hyundai Motor Finance.
Speaker 1 explains the payment grid, showing different permutations across lease terms, loan terms, rates, and rebates.
Carmatic helps dealers maximize profit by considering both front-end and back-end gross, applying rules pertaining to lenders or deal structures.
Profit Maximization and Credit Structures
Carmatic automatically selects the best possible option available for dealers, considering various credit structures and scores.
The system updates payments as credit scores change and applies consumer-facing cash available on the market.
Conditional incentives like first responder or military discounts are automatically considered, along with stackability and combineability rules.
More information about deal structures is available, including incentive stack, residuals, money factors, lender-based fees, APR, and total amount financed.
Tax and Title Calculations
Carmatic uses low precision taxes based on the deal's calculation before it's completed, updating jurisdictional-level fees once the deal is finalized.
The system operates based on the consumer's location, updating programs, incentives, taxes, titles, and licenses automatically.
Dealers can choose to add F&I products, mark them up, or default them, including care packages in the initial first pencil.
Recommendation APIs provide multiple modes, such as profit, to find similar vehicles with comparable feature sets and payments.
Finalizing Deals and System Capabilities
Carmatic allows one-click building of deals without starting from scratch, providing a quick overview of the system and its capabilities.
The system offers various modes for recommendations, including profit, to find alternate options for consumers.
Carmatic's recommendation API helps dealers build deals efficiently, considering different vehicle types like new, used, CPO, or in-transit vehicles.
The system's capabilities are summarized, thanking the audience for their time.
Basic GTM Information
Core Functionality
Carmatic offers a dealer-facing shop-by-payment experience powered by a comprehensive API suite. It delivers real-time, transactional payment calculations based on dealer-configured structures, incentives, consumer credit data, equity, and vehicle inventory.
Key Differentiators & Value Drivers
The Real Value of Carmatic
Carmatic is a digital F&I strategist built into the front of the sales process. It takes guesswork out of payment quoting and maximizes dealer profit by optimizing structures invisible to most salespeople.
Think:
"If Informativ Compliance is your shield against risk, Carmatic is your sword for profit optimization."
This is especially valuable in today’s market, where:
Margins are shrinking
Consumers demand transparency
Regulations are tightening
Speed-to-close is essential
Go-To-Market (GTM) Strategy for Carmatic + Informativ
Target Audience
Franchise auto groups with multiple rooftops
Independent high-volume dealers
Digital-first dealer groups
BDC or internet sales operations
Compliance-conscious, profit-driven GMs
Key Messaging Pillars
“More Gross. Less Guess.”
Automatically quote the best structure for each buyer – lease or loan – while maximizing profit.
“From Estimate to Exact.”
Ditch payment calculators and deliver real deal terms with lender-specific rules and real-time TTL.
“Stackable Profits, Not Stip Piles.”
Automatically apply every rebate, incentive, or markup rule while managing equity and LTV intelligently.
“Accelerate Every Deal.”
Reduce desking from 20 minutes to under 2 with auto-configured deal builds.
“Built for Compliance. Tuned for Profit.”
Perfect pairing with Informativ Compliance and Credit products. Complete the journey from lead to funded deal.
Sample Tiered Bundle Pitch with Carmatic
The Only Complete and Proactive Fraud Prevention, Compliance, Credit, and Customer Insights Platform
GTM Tactics
Launch Focus
Introduce during Solution Fit or Tech Demo as a profit accelerator tied to desking.
Use deal calculator comparisons during demo to show front/back-end profit lift with Carmatic vs traditional desking.
Target compliance + finance combo pain points: "Compliance is a must. Profit shouldn’t suffer."
Enablement
Quote Carmatic in CPQ as a line-item or pre-bundled solution for Tier I / Tier II
Include it in desking pain point scenarios during solution fit (e.g., high turnover F&I departments, slow desking, digital retailing confusion)
Use Profit-per-deal calculator from ROI tools to show lift
Ideal Integrations
CRM: VIN Solutions, eLeads, DealerSocket
DMS: Reynolds & Reynolds (post-export), DealerTrack
Informativ Customer Insights: Use credit tier data to auto-filter Carmatic payments
Informativ Compliance: Ensure all deals comply with FTC pricing transparency and structure rules

“Imagine every sales manager quoting real payments instantly, maximizing profit, and never needing to re-desk a deal. That’s Carmatic. When you combine it with Informativ’s Credit and Compliance platform, you’ve just gone from reactive to revenue-positive – with every click.”

True transactional payments (not estimates) | Boosts consumer confidence, improves conversion rates, and sets it apart from most online retailing tools

THE TIERED PRODUCT STORY
1. FOUNDATION
Tagline: Credit Reports Wherever You Need Them
Who It’s For: Dealerships that only need straightforward credit pulls through their preferred platform (i.e. – RouteOne, Dealertrack)—no extra frills, but does include a website credit application for consumers to pre-qualify and/or complete a full credit application on dealer’s websites.
Value Message: Reliable, flat-rate credit reports with a flexible credit bank that scales with your dealership’s needs, giving you the freedom to pull credit where and when you want.
Key Features:
No-nonsense credit reports
Pull credit from any supported system
Flexible, refillable credit bank
Simple, flat-rate pricing
2. DETECT
Tagline: Credit Reports with Essential Compliance & Fraud Protection
Who It’s For: Dealers that don’t yet need showroom-wide compliance controls but want to reduce risk on remote and online deals with basic compliance and fraud safeguards.  Compliance offering to meet credit regulatory requirements.
Value Message: Affordable credit pricing combined with built-in credit based compliance and fraud prevention features, helping you protect your dealership and your customers without overcomplicating your process.
Key Features:
Red Flags Hard Stop
Credit compliance tools
Integrated fraud detection for credit pulls
Soft pull and/or hard pull credit reports
3. PROTECT
Tagline: Enhanced Compliance & Showroom Guardrails
Who It’s For: Dealerships ready to take control of their in-store and remote compliance processes with advanced fraud prevention, digital document management, and enforceable workflows customized to meet each dealership’s current process.
Value Message: Achieve a compliant, paperless sales process with robust proactive compliance enforcement and fraud protection, ensuring every deal—remote or in-person—follows a consistent, defensible process.
Key Features:
Paperless sales process with e-signatures
Digital deal jacket creation and secure storage
Enforceable compliant workflows with enforced process stops and real time notifications of potential violations
Fraud detection in both showroom and remote environments
4. CONTROL
Tagline: Optimized and Controlled Credit Usage & Enhanced Customer Insights
Who It’s For: Dealers seeking to manage credit spend, minimize compliance burdens, and gain actionable insights to sell more efficiently and effectively.
Value Message: Drive smarter sales and stronger margins by controlling credit spend, integrating soft pulls to reduce compliance obligations, and leveraging real-time customer insights to close more deals faster.
Key Features:
Showroom credit spend controls and optimization
Seamless soft pull workflows to reduce compliance burden
Eliminate customer friction and improve buying experience
Speed up the process at the desk
Customer Insights dashboard to identify buying potential and improve closing rates
Tech to minimize compliance friction while maximizing opportunity

testimonials.label: Customer Video Testimonials
testimonials.description: 42 video testimonials from dealers including Autobahn BMW, Pohanka Auto Group, Cavender Auto Family, Kia Moritz, Kunes Auto Group, Boucher Auto Group, Galpin Motors, Gay Family Auto, Crest Automotive, Firkins Auto Group, Toyota of Cedar Park — covering fraud prevention, compliance, customer experience, cost savings, soft pulls, SmartPencil, pre-desking, and more. Each includes a link to the video on informativ.com.

chunks.source: Video Testimonial #2: Kunes Auto Group Accelerates Without Cutting Corners
chunks.content:
VIDEO TESTIMONIAL: Kunes Auto Group Accelerates Without Cutting Corners
Speaker: Scott Kunes, Chief Operating Officer
Company: Kunes Auto Group
Location: Wisconsin and Michigan
[Watch the Video](https://informativ.com/kunes-auto-group-accelerates-without-cutting-corners)
Topics: Compliance & Guardrails, Process Consistency, Multi-Location / Enterprise, Scalable Across Stores, Audit Scores & Readiness

Transcript:
"Typically when you speed up a process like this or try to institute a process, everybody knows the salesperson working the deal is running up saying, I need a trade appraise, I need a credit app, I need this, I need this, I need this. And you want to slow that process down, and it's extremely important so that you don't get sloppy or skip steps, right? With Informativ, we're doing it the right way every time. And it's really critical to us with 40 locations and 1,500 employees to have that process that we can manage, watch, and audit, and make sure that it's happening over and over again."
chunks.speaker: Scott Kunes, Chief Operating Officer

chunks.source: Video Testimonial #3: IDEA Auto Group Streamlines Credit Usage and Saves Time
chunks.content:
VIDEO TESTIMONIAL: IDEA Auto Group Streamlines Credit Usage and Saves Time
Speaker: Rigo Guevara, General Manager
Company: Toyota of Cedar Park
Location: Cedar Park, Texas
[Watch the Video](https://informativ.com/case-studies/idea-auto-group-streamlines-credit-usage-and-saves-time/)
Topics: Soft Pull Prequalification, Soft Pull Credit, Customer Experience & Trust, Competing with Credit Unions, Competitive Advantage

Transcript:
"What the prequals have done for us is take the customer into consideration, right? It creates a better experience for an inquiry. It's a hard pull, and if we can eliminate some of that and create a better experience for the customer, they notice. And in our market, we have a lot of credit unions that we're constantly competing with. With the pre-qual, we're able to be better informed and often compete with the rate that the customer is shopping. So it allows us to do that without the big commitment from the customer."
chunks.speaker: Rigo Guevara, General Manager

chunks.source: Video Testimonial #4: Kia Moritz's Paperless Process Improves the Customer Experience
chunks.content:
VIDEO TESTIMONIAL: Kia Moritz's Paperless Process Improves the Customer Experience
Speaker: Lynn Crabtree, Customer
Company: Kia Moritz Alliance
Location: Fort Worth, Texas
[Watch the Video](https://informativ.com/case-studies/kia-moritzs-paperless-process-improves-the-customer-experience/)
Topics: Customer Experience & Trust, Paperless Showroom, Digital / Paperless Process, Building Customer Trust, Customer Data Protection

Transcript:
"I am actually a customer at Kia Moritz. I was shopping for a car with my brother, and we went to several different dealerships. I really liked coming here because at the other dealerships, you come in and there was a lot of paperwork. They wanted to take a picture of the driver's license, and it just made me feel uneasy because you didn't know where it was going. So when I came here, it seemed like they had things put together because right away they scanned the driver's license right there in front of me. And then when we went through everything, it was all digitalized. So it seemed easy, and it felt like they had things put together in a way that made me trust them a little bit more than other dealerships."
chunks.speaker: Lynn Crabtree, Customer

chunks.source: Video Testimonial #5: Informativ Helps Pohanka Auto Group Build a More Confident, Consistent Sales Process
chunks.content:
VIDEO TESTIMONIAL: Informativ Helps Pohanka Auto Group Build a More Confident, Consistent Sales Process
Speaker: Jimmy Robinson, Chief Financial Officer
Company: Pohanka Auto Group
Location: Washington D.C., Maryland, Virginia, and Texas
[Watch the Video](https://informativ.com/confident-consistent-sales-with-informativ/)
Topics: Compliance & Guardrails, Process Consistency, sales_confidence, Customer Experience & Trust

Transcript:
"The consistency comes from having clear expectations, and that makes your dealership run smoother. The sales associate understands the expectations put on them, and the desk has those same expectations. Then the customer experience improves. It's very clear, and the software helps promote that level of confidence in everybody."
chunks.speaker: Jimmy Robinson, Chief Financial Officer

chunks.source: Video Testimonial #6: Cavender Auto Praises Informativ's Unmatched Partnership
chunks.content:
VIDEO TESTIMONIAL: Cavender Auto Praises Informativ's Unmatched Partnership
Speaker: Ben Stillwagon, Director of IT
Company: Cavender Auto Family
Location: Texas
[Watch the Video](https://informativ.com/case-studies/cavender-auto-praises-informativs-unmatched-partnership/)
Topics: Informativ Partnership & Support, Customer Service, Customer Support

Transcript:
"Really, the team has been great in understanding our business and thinking of ways to make us better and check all the boxes. I think what it really boils down to is the team. You guys have a great team here."
chunks.speaker: Ben Stillwagon, Director of IT

chunks.source: Video Testimonial #7: Kia Moritz Earns Customer Confidence from the Start
chunks.content:
VIDEO TESTIMONIAL: Kia Moritz Earns Customer Confidence from the Start
Speaker: Dejan Siljegovic, Sales Manager
Company: Kia Moritz of Alliance
Location: Fort Worth, Texas
[Watch the Video](https://informativ.com/case-studies/kia-moritz-earns-customer-confidence-from-the-start/)
Topics: Customer Experience & Trust, Customer Data Protection, Digital / Paperless Process, Building Customer Trust, Credit Score Protection

Transcript:
"I think the customer gains a bit of confidence seeing that their information is not all over the place or spread across desks. There's nothing to print aside from a couple of forms that we have internally. Everything goes through an iPad and gets pushed through the system. Especially here, with credit ratings being so important, people who have established their credit want to keep it that way. I think it inspires confidence in us for them and makes it much easier for them to do business with us today and in the future because of that."
chunks.speaker: Dejan Siljegovic, Sales Manager

chunks.content:
VIDEO TESTIMONIAL: Managers Love Informativ
Speaker: Doug Ashbrook, General Sales Manager
Company: Kia Moritz of Alliance
Location: Fort Worth, Texas
[Watch the Video](https://informativ.com/case-studies/managers-love-informativ/)
Topics: Compliance & Guardrails, Enforceable Compliance Guardrails, Faster Sales Process, Manager Protection

Transcript:
"I would say the biggest thing is the whole safety process and being compliant. It's made it really easy for us because if anything is wrong, anything that pops up gives us a stop sign and we can't move forward. I like it. For manager protection, Informativ is a great process to keep you compliant and to speed up the process between the customer and the desk."
chunks.speaker: Doug Ashbrook, General Sales Manager

chunks.source: Video Testimonial #9: Kunes Auto Group Safeguards Customer Data
chunks.content:
VIDEO TESTIMONIAL: Kunes Auto Group Safeguards Customer Data
Speaker: Scott Kunes, Chief Operating Officer

They already know what they're doing, and they're going to tell you that they know what they're doing—and they're probably not going to want any help on that. But it's about raising the bar across the group. Unfortunately, in the car business, there's a lot of turnover, and that's no different at our stores, no matter how much we try to combat that. So as we're training new desk managers, it's important for us to make sure we're taking advantage of all the tools we have possible to do that. When they can see the full spectrum of lenders—when we're talking about 30-plus lenders, and 100-plus when you add CUDL into there—and the constantly changing bank parameters, it's a lot to manage. We try to stay on top of it. You try to make sure your entire group is talking about it. VIDEO TESTIMONIAL: Autobahn BMW Sells More Cars with Confidence and Efficiency
Speaker: Brandon Lee, New Car Sales Director
Company: Autobahn BMW
Location: Fort Worth, Texas
[Watch the Video](https://informativ.com/case-studies/how-autobahn-bmw-sells-more-cars-with-speed-and-confidence/)
Topics: Easy to Use, Desk Manager Visibility, Sales Team Adoption, Process Speed, Fraud Prevention

Transcript:
"It's made us so much more efficient. My sales managers and desk managers absolutely love the technology. Whenever you're desking a deal, you can see everything—you can see that the driver's license is scanned, the insurance is scanned, the OFAC, the red flags, the soft pull. It allows me, as a manager, to desk my deals correctly. It allows us to be more efficient in every way, and that's what it's about nowadays. When you're trying to grow and sell more cars, you need to be as efficient as possible—and you need to do that with the confidence that you're moving forward with a legitimate customer."
chunks.speaker: Brandon Lee, New Car Sales Director

chunks.source: Video Testimonial #41: Cavender Auto Family Delivers Instant Transparency with Informativ
chunks.content:
VIDEO TESTIMONIAL: Cavender Auto Family Delivers Instant Transparency with Informativ
Speaker: Ben Stillwagon, Director of IT
Company: Cavender Auto Family
Location: Texas and Oklahoma
[Watch the Video](https://informativ.com/case-studies/cavender-auto-family-soft-pull/)
Topics: SmartPencil, Customer Experience, Soft Pull / Prequalification, Pre-Desking, Transparency

Transcript:
"SmartPencil offers it all right there at the beginning of the deal, right? Our customers, they want transparency, and they want it pretty much right when they walk in—and it provides that. It provides just from a simple soft pull, you get a payment option. You get all your options right there in front that you can present to the customers."
chunks.speaker: Ben Stillwagon, Director of IT
